positionName: Senior Order Operations Analyst, Client Facing
description:
This role requires the ability to work lawfully in the U.S. without employment-based immigration sponsorship, now or in the future.

Are you an Operations Analyst with an understanding of Spectrum Business systems and tools, policies, and order lifecycle, and a high level of organizational skills with an ability to effectively manage accounts with complex products and multiple locations? You can do that. Ready to serve as a senior member of the team who mentors, trains and serves as back up to the team Lead? As a Sr. Order Operations Analyst at Spectrum Business, you can do that.

Spectrum Business provides modern enterprise technology solutions that meet the unique needs of some of the country’s biggest brands. If you’re looking to build your most successful career, support client growth and work alongside intelligent, driven professionals, you can do that. We're ready to go all in on your future and create an engaging environment.

Be part of the connection:

As a Senior Order Operations Analyst, you are responsible for analyzing large, complex orders post Sale Complete and processing accounts in a timely and accurate fashion. You are accountable for delivering accurate data and administration concerning order to cash processes.

How you can make a difference:

Specialize in performing post-sale and order entry activities for Voice and complex, high value orders.
Matrix managed efforts to ensure SLOs are maintained, and that service implementation is executed.
Engage clients to clarify order details, including contract revisions, phone port details, and regulatory voice documents.
Understanding of the billing process including pro-rates, non-pay, rate increases, adjustments, credits, discounts, collections, renewals, and billing equations.
Senior member of team; backup team Lead(s).
Partner with LECs to resolve order issues and obtain FOC.

WHAT YOU BRING TO SPECTRUM BUSINESS

Required qualifications:

Experience: Three or more years of Customer service, order entry; Two years Advanced telephony experience; Two years Salesforce; Two years Telecommunications
Education: Associate degree (A.A.) or equivalent from two-year college or technical school; or one-year related experience and/or training; or equivalent combination of education and experience.
Skills:
Proficient in all Order Operations processes and business rules
Organization, prioritization and decision-making skills
Knowledge of MS Office
Understanding of Spectrum’s product and service offerings
Abilities:
Work effectively in a team-oriented environment
Maintain confidentiality
Build working relationships
Mentor and motivate others
Meet deadlines

#LI-EW1
SSU306 2026-73013 2026

Here, our employees don’t just have jobs, they're building careers. That’s why we offer a comprehensive pay and benefits package that rewards employees for their contributions to our success, supporting all aspects of their well-being at every stage of life.

A qualified applicant’s criminal history, if any, will be considered in a manner consistent with applicable laws, including local ordinances.

Get to Know Us Charter Communications provides superior communication and entertainment products for residential and business customers through the Spectrum brand. Our offerings include Spectrum Internet®, TV, Mobile and Voice. Beyond our connectivity solutions, we also provide local news, programming and regional sports via Spectrum Networks and multiscreen advertising solutions via Spectrum Reach. When you join our team, you’ll be keeping our customers connected to what matters most in 41 states across the U.S. Watch this video to learn more.

Grow Your Career Here We’re committed to growing a workforce that reflects the customers and communities we serve – providing opportunities for employment and advancement to all team members. Spectrum is an Equal Opportunity Employer, including job seekers with disabilities and veterans. Learn about Life at Spectrum.

enrichment.role_title: Senior Order Operations Analyst, Client Facing
enrichment.score_rationale: The role has highly repeatable order-entry and billing workflows common across telecom companies, but client engagement, mentoring, and judgment-heavy order resolution reduce automation feasibility. Phone/voice duties (port details, LEC coordination, client engagement) are explicitly central, and only one named tool (Salesforce) is mentioned alongside generic MS Office.
core_tasks: Process post-sale and order entry for Voice and complex high-value orders
Analyze large complex orders post Sale Complete for accuracy
Engage clients to clarify order details including contract revisions and phone port details
Manage regulatory voice documents and phone porting
Understand and apply billing processes including pro-rates, credits, discounts, and renewals
Matrix manage efforts to ensure SLOs are maintained
Partner with LECs to resolve order issues and obtain FOC
Serve as senior team member and backup to team Lead(s)
Mentor and train junior team members

description:
*Job Summary*

We are a manufacturing supplier to the window and door industry seeking a dynamic individual for Order Entry / Customer Service Representative. We are looking for someone who likes a challenging, fast-paced manufacturing environment, where they will communicate regularly with internal and external customers to ensure that everyone receives the best standards of customer care.

*Summary of essential job functions:*

· Answers incoming customer calls and emails regarding order status, ship dates, product information, pricing or other information

· Process incoming customer Purchase Orders

· Review orders for accuracy and completion

· Attend daily production meetings for manufacturing, purchasing, and shipping updates

· Communicate with sales team regarding customer requirements as needed

· Assist walk in customers with placing orders and processing COD payments

· Review daily Order Report to expedite shipments and contact customers to arrange pickup of materials

· Invoice shipped goods daily

· Initiate Customer Complaint process and manage customer expectations

· Process credit card machine nightly

· Support outside sales team by entering and sending quotes to potential opportunities and other tasks as required

*Qualifications and Skills:*

· 2 yrs+ Manufacturing experience required

· 2 yrs+ customer service experience and data entry experience required

· Microsoft Office Suite experience required

· Experience with ERP/ Data Entry software required

· College Diploma Preferred

· Proficient in the English Language essential, Bilingual preferred (French)

· Attention to detail

· Strong organizational skills

· Strong verbal and written communication skills

· Able to work as a team player in a fast paced environment

*Job Duties:*

* Answer incoming customer inquiries
* Collaborate with key stakeholders and teams to stay updated on new products, services, and policies
* Record and modify customer information within the database
* Engage with clients in a friendly and professional manner while actively listening to their concerns
* Offer support and solutions to customers in accordance with the company's customer service policies
* Other duties as requested

*Disclaimer*

The above statements are intended to describe the general nature and level of work being performed by people assigned to this classification. They are not to be construed as an exhaustive list of all responsibilities, duties, and skills required of personnel so classified. All personnel may be required to perform duties outside of their normal responsibilities from time to time, as needed.

Job Type: Part-time

Pay: $24.00 - $26.00 per hour



Experience:
* Order entry: 2 years (Required)
* Manufacturing: 2 years (Required)
* Customer service: 2 years (Required)

Language:
* English (Required)

Ability to Commute:
* Dallas, TX 75247 (Required)

Work Location: In person

enrichment.score_rationale: While order entry, invoicing, and daily reporting are highly repetitive and automatable, inbound phone calls and walk-in customer interactions are a core and primary duty, dragging down the score significantly. The listing only vaguely references an ERP system without naming a specific platform, and at $24–$26/hr part-time it falls below the $50K/yr full-time equivalent threshold.

description:
Interested in a career that matters?

Interested in a career that matters?

At Novonesis, we partner closely with customers to deliver biological solutions that make a real difference. As part of our Customer Service team, you will play a key role in ensuring smooth order processing and timely deliveries while acting as a trusted liaison between customers and internal teams.

You will work in a collaborative, customer‑focused environment where accuracy, responsiveness, and professionalism directly contribute to customer satisfaction and business success.

The Role & Your Impact

This role is primarily operational and focuses on managing customer orders and related documentation to ensure on‑time, accurate deliveries. You will support both domestic and international customers and collaborate closely with Supply, Sales, Shipping, and Warehouse teams.

Key responsibilities include:

Processing domestic and international customer orders in SAP, ensuring accuracy in pricing, availability, and delivery requirements
Coordinating with Supply, Sales, and Shipping to resolve product availability or delivery issues
Creating deliveries and supporting warehouse and shipping teams within agreed timeframes
Supporting customer service requests and complaints, including documentation, SAP case creation, and return shipments
Providing high‑quality customer support via phone and email, troubleshooting issues and proposing solutions
Running daily reports and applying double‑check processes to ensure on‑time delivery and order accuracy
Maintaining accurate customer master data in SAP
Supporting department goals, KPIs, and continuous improvement initiatives

What You’ll Need to Be Successful

Must‑haves:

High School Diploma or GED
Minimum 1 year of customer service experience
Professional written and verbal communication skills in English
Ability to work independently and as part of a team with strong accountability and sense of urgency
Ability to multitask and work with multiple systems and dual monitors
Working knowledge of Microsoft Office and SAP (or similar ERP systems)
Basic understanding of logistics and manufacturing processes
Ability to perform calculations and conversions (e.g. pricing, units, quantities)

Additional Skills & Interests We Value

Nice‑to‑haves:

Experience supporting international customers or export documentation
Prior experience working with logistics providers, freight forwarders, or shipping documentation
Experience handling customer complaints or returns processes
Interest in continuous improvement and preventative customer service practices

What We Offer

At Novonesis, you will join a purpose‑driven company with a strong focus on collaboration and customer impact. We offer:

A supportive and team‑oriented work environment
Opportunities to develop customer service, logistics, and system skills
A culture that values accountability, quality, and continuous improvement

Ways of Working / Working Conditions

This role is based in an office environment with generally favorable working conditions.
The position involves:

Primarily sedentary work with standing or walking up to approximately 10% of the workday
Standard office lighting, temperature, and noise levels
Rotational on‑call work as required to support customer needs

Compensation & Benefits

Expected salary: $23/hr

Benefits you will enjoy:

401(k) with up to a 9% company contribution
Minimum of 3 weeks’ vacation, plus 12 holidays and 2 weeks of wellness time
Health, Dental, Vision & Life insurance
Healthcare savings account option with generous employer contribution
Employee Assistance Program
Parental leave
Tuition reimbursement
All benefits begin on your first day

Our purpose guides the way

In Novonesis, we know that solutions rooted in biology can help solve humanity’s biggest challenges. Since we began more than a century ago, this has been our guide. It’s how we've gotten so far. And it’s how we'll impact the future. Now, more than ever, the world needs change. And with biosolutions, the possibilities for transformation are endless. If our purpose resonates with you, we encourage you to apply.

Opportunities for everyone

We are committed to an inclusive recruitment process and equal opportunity for all applicants. Therefore we ask you to apply without a cover letter or photo and instead include a few sentences in your CV explaining your motivation for applying.

We make all employment decisions based on business needs and welcome candidates with a wide range of backgrounds, including ethnicity, religion, gender, sexual orientation, age, disability, or veteran status.

Explore other openings

If this position isn’t the right fit, you may still find other opportunities with us. You’re welcome to explore open roles or create a job agent to stay connected with future opportunities at novonesis.com/Career

description:
The Customer Service Associate under strict supervision, ensures accurate and timely processing of orders and related requisitions.

Pay Rate: Up to $24.00/hour, depending on experience.
Assignment Length: 2-year contract with a high possibility of extension and/or conversion based on performance and other factors.

Responsibilities:

Acts as a contact with affiliates, field PSL, and sales personnel.
May create invoices and shipping documentation.
Facilitates movements of materials to support PSL operations.
Performs data inquiries to obtain order status and check availability of materials.
Serves as a liaison for communication with field personnel, manufacturing, procurement, etc. to fill customer requirements.
Coordinates cross-center and vendor supplied orders as required and monitors those shipments.
Works with Law Department to ensure export compliance and stays current regarding international material movements and shipping regulations.

Qualifications:

High School Diploma or GED required.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
Proficient in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, Outlook).
Strong organizational and time-management abilities.

Halliburton is an innovative leader in the oilfield services industry, providing products and services for the ever-evolving needs of the energy sector.

Since starting out over a hundred years ago, Halliburton’s path to success has been built on growth. Today, with over 50,000 employees worldwide and 1500 new openings every year, our talent needs have never been greater.

That’s why Halliburton has partnered with Talent Solutions to meet their ongoing contingent staffing needs. If you want to grow your career alongside an industry leader that is changing the world of energy, then we look forward to connecting with you inside our Halliburton Contingent Workforce Network – your gateway to meaningful work opportunities all in one place.

#HALL24

description:
Join a team where passion meets purpose! At Bodhi, we’re not just filling a role — we’re inviting someone who thrives in a fast-paced, values-driven environment to help shape the future of cannabis manufacturing. If you’re ready to roll up your sleeves, bring fresh ideas, and grow with a company that’s as committed to excellence as it is to its people, this is your opportunity to make an impact. Keep reading to see if this role is your perfect fit.

*Position Description*

_Fulfillment Inventory & Returns Specialist_

*Status*

_Full-Time, Non- Exempt_

*Department*

_Fulfillment_

*Reports to*

_Fulfillment Manager_

*Why Bodhi Brands?*

We believe business should serve people, not the other way around. At Bodhi Brands, we’re not just building products; we’re growing a purpose-driven company that empowers individuals, nurtures authentic community, and leads with heart. Our mission is to redefine what success looks like in business by listening to a deeper purpose, growing people, giving radically, and shaping a more compassionate world. If you're seeking a place to grow, serve, and lead with intention you might've just found your people.

*Who We Are*

We’re a passionate team of makers, thinkers, and doers with big hearts and bold visions. We lead with integrity, collaborate with humility, and challenge each other to keep growing. We value feedback, growth, and the kind of leadership that starts with how you show up, no matter your title. We’re not here to simply clock in, we're here to co-create something meaningful.

*What Difference We Make in the World*

We exist to spark transformation in individuals, in teams, and in the world around us. By leading with values like service-driven leadership, deep listening, growth mindset, and whole-life wellbeing, we’re working to redefine what business can be. Through coaching, community work, and courageous conversations, we’re building a culture that develops leaders and drives meaningful social change from the inside out.

*What It’s Like to Be on Our Team*

Bodhi is a place to be real, grow fast, and do meaningful work. We laugh hard, lift each other through challenges, and celebrate the wins, big and small. We show up with care, curiosity, and a deep sense of shared responsibility. Our team is rooted in purpose, driven by a growth mindset, and united by the desire to build something that truly matters.

*Core Values*

Humbly Confident - Trusted Teammate - Service Driven - Strategic Innovation -

Growth Mindset

*Job Summary*

The Fulfillment Inventory & Returns Specialist is responsible for maintaining accurate inventory records, processing product returns, and ensuring compliance with traceability and quality standards. This role serves as a critical link between fulfillment, inventory control, and quality assurance by managing discrepancies, returns, and product flow integrity.

*Disclaimer:*

_This job description is intended to outline the core duties and qualifications of the role. It is not exhaustive and may be subject to change or revision. Employees may be required to perform other related duties as assigned, in support of the department or organization's goals, even if such tasks are not specifically listed in this document._

*Key Responsibilities*

*5 Core Responsibilities:*

* *Maintain Inventory Accuracy and Traceability*
* *Process Returns, Corrections, and Product Reconciliation*
* *Monitor and Resolve Inventory Discrepancies*
* *Ensure Compliance with Inventory and Regulatory Standards*
* *Support Cross-Functional Inventory Flow and Continuous Improvement*

*Key Duties & Expectations:*

*Inventory Management*

* Perform routine cycle counts and full inventory audits.
* Maintain accurate inventory records across all systems (e.g., Cultivera, spreadsheets).
* Reconcile physical inventory against digital records and resolve discrepancies.
* Monitor inventory levels and flag shortages, overages, or anomalies.
* Ensure proper bin placement, labeling, and storage standards are followed.

*Returns & Corrections*

* Process returned orders, including inspection, documentation, and system adjustments.
* Investigate root causes of returns (fulfillment error, damage, compliance issue, etc.).
* Coordinate with QA and Fulfillment to determine product disposition (restock, rework, destroy).
* Maintain detailed return logs and ensure proper documentation for compliance.
* Execute rework or relabeling processes when required.

*Compliance & Documentation*

* Ensure all inventory movements meet i502/WAC compliance standards.
* Maintain audit-ready documentation for inventory adjustments and returns.
* Assist with internal and external audits by providing accurate records.
* Follow all SOPs related to inventory handling, traceability, and product control.

*Cross-Functional Coordination*

* Work closely with Fulfillment, Packaging, QA, and Sales teams to resolve inventory issues.
* Communicate discrepancies, delays, or risks to leadership in real time.
* Support order packing on high volume days.

*Continuous Improvement*

* Identify trends in returns and inventory errors and recommend process improvements.
* Support Lean initiatives such as 5S, Standard Work, and root cause analysis.
* Contribute to improving inventory workflows and system accuracy.

*Key Duties & Expectations:*

* Leads, Manages, and holds Accountable (LMA) Fulfillment Leads and Assistants.
* Supervises daily fulfillment operations and ensures timely, accurate order processing.
* Assigns tasks and tracks progress using systems like S2S or Cultivera.
* Coordinates with Packaging for product intake and order building.
* Manages inventory, audits, and documentation (manifests, invoices, vendor communication).
* Simplifies workflow for the team and provides real-time coaching.
* Handles training, timesheets, and issue escalation.
* Predicts workflow gaps and adjusts team scheduling and priorities accordingly.
* Supervise daily order fulfillment tasks, including assigning duties and overseeing progress.
* Audit and manage inventory, coordinate closely with packaging for product intake.
* Track and organize orders via systems such as S2S/Cultivera and sales spreadsheets.
* Build, sticker, and audit orders for shipment; ensure traceability and accuracy.
* Support delivery coordination and documentation: manifests, invoices, and test results.
* Oversee team timesheets, productivity, and training.
* Handle customer and Sale Rep, communications regarding orders and delivery details.
* Maintain a clean and organized inventory and fulfillment workspace.
* Manage returned products, adjust inventories, and process credits when necessary
* Coordinate delivery logistics, including in-house and third-party transportation.
* Maintain and audit inventory, update menus, and manage returned product workflows.
* Support the sales team with data input, reports, inventory adjustments, and sample routes.
* Drives operational improvements using Lean tools such as 5S, Standard Work, Visual Management, and Kaizen events.
* Actively contributes to process flow improvements by identifying inefficiencies, leading root cause analysis, and supporting cross-functional problem-solving.
* Helps maintain steady, balanced workflows by minimizing downtime, flexing labor across tasks, and coordinating with upstream and downstream departments.

*Qualifications*

* Understanding of production and the process of preparing products for retail.
* Proficient computer skills. Cultivera knowledge is a plus!
* Excellent interpersonal skills, communication skills and self-motivation.
* Oral & written communication skills.
* Professional appearance and presentation required.
* General understanding of the function and operation of packaging, shipping & receiving processes at Bodhi.
* Knowledge and understanding of i502 laws and regulation to ensure compliance.
* Strong attention to detail and organizational skills
* Experience with inventory reconciliation and discrepancy resolution
* Ability to analyze problems and identify root causes
* Proficiency in spreadsheets and inventory tracking systems
* Effective communication and cross-functional collaboration
* Ability to handle sensitive or regulated product with care and accuracy

*Working conditions*

* Environment: Indoor warehouse/production facility.
* Dress Code: Packagers are required to adhere to the company dress code policy.
* Conditions: Variable temperatures, strong odors, noise exposure.
* Hygiene: Strict sanitation and product safety compliance required.

*Physical requirements*

* Stand and walk for extended periods (up to 8–10 hours per shift).
* Perform repetitive hand & arm movements, twisting, gripping, and fine motor tasks.
* Lift, push, or pull up to 40 lbs. frequently. Occasionally up to 50 lbs. with assistance.
* Bend, squat, reach, and kneel throughout the shift as needed.
* Work with urgency and attention to detail while maintaining accuracy and safety.
* Tolerate exposure to strong odors, variable room temperatures, and continuous noise from machinery and production equipment.
* Wear required personal protective equipment (PPE) for the duration of the shift (e.g., gloves, masks, lab coats, hairnets).

*Electronic Communication & Systems Requirements*

Effective communication and timely responsiveness are critical to team success. All team members are expected to:

* *Check and respond to work-related emails regularly* for shift updates, announcements, and compliance notifications.
* *Utilize Basecamp* for task management, project collaboration, scheduling, and departmental communication.
* *Log in to designated HR platform* to manage personal employee information, time-off requests, and company-wide updates.

All team members must maintain an active account in each system, review updates daily during scheduled work periods, and complete assigned digital training or forms as required.

*Disclaimer*

The information presented indicates the general nature and level of work expected for the described position above. It is not designed to contain, nor to be interpreted as, a comprehensive inventory of all duties, responsibilities, qualifications and objectives required of employees assigned to this position. Rather, they are intended only to describe the general nature of the position.

_*Bodhi Core Values*_

*Humbly Confident*

We lead with quiet confidence, acknowledging our strengths while remaining open to feedback and growth. We take initiative, act professionally, and push through challenges with determination. Ego is left at the door, and humility paves the way for authentic leadership.

*Trusted Teammate*

We solve problems before they grow, follow through on every commitment, and show up for our team with consistency, honor and care. We earn trust by being dependable, detail-oriented, and always ready to step in and step up.

*Service Driven*

We are committed to serving others — customers, teammates, and our community. Every action we take is driven by a mindset of adding value and making a positive impact. We show up and help build a culture of support, generosity, and shared success.

*Strategic Innovation*

We don’t just solve problems — we reimagine what’s possible. We think creatively, act strategically, develop thoughtful products, and seek smarter ways to work, grow, and lead. We improve what exists and envision what’s next.

*Growth Mindset*

We believe in continuous improvement and the power of learning. Challenges are opportunities for growth. We take initiative by seeking out greater responsibility — not just to advance our own development, but to strengthen the team as a whole.

Job Type: Full-time

Pay: $18.00 - $25.00 per hour

Benefits:
* Paid sick time
* Paid time off



Application Question(s):
* Employees must be a minimum of 21 years of age to work in the Cannabis industry.  Are you over 21 years of age?

Ability to Commute:
* Spokane Valley, WA 99212 (Preferred)

Work Location: In person
descriptionHTML:
Join a team where passion meets purpose! At Bodhi, we’re not just filling a role — we’re inviting someone who thrives in a fast-paced, values-driven environment to help shape the future of cannabis manufacturing. If you’re ready to roll up your sleeves, bring fresh ideas, and grow with a company that’s as committed to excellence as it is to its people, this is your opportunity to make an impact. Keep reading to see if this role is your perfect fit.

Position Description

Fulfillment Inventory & Returns Specialist

Status

Full-Time, Non- Exempt

Department

Fulfillment

Reports to

Fulfillment Manager

Why Bodhi Brands?

We believe business should serve people, not the other way around. At Bodhi Brands, we’re not just building products; we’re growing a purpose-driven company that empowers individuals, nurtures authentic community, and leads with heart. Our mission is to redefine what success looks like in business by listening to a deeper purpose, growing people, giving radically, and shaping a more compassionate world. If you're seeking a place to grow, serve, and lead with intention you might've just found your people.

Who We Are

We’re a passionate team of makers, thinkers, and doers with big hearts and bold visions. We lead with integrity, collaborate with humility, and challenge each other to keep growing. We value feedback, growth, and the kind of leadership that starts with how you show up, no matter your title. We’re not here to simply clock in, we're here to co-create something meaningful.

What Difference We Make in the World

We exist to spark transformation in individuals, in teams, and in the world around us. By leading with values like service-driven leadership, deep listening, growth mindset, and whole-life wellbeing, we’re working to redefine what business can be. Through coaching, community work, and courageous conversations, we’re building a culture that develops leaders and drives meaningful social change from the inside out.

What It’s Like to Be on Our Team

Bodhi is a place to be real, grow fast, and do meaningful work. We laugh hard, lift each other through challenges, and celebrate the wins, big and small. We show up with care, curiosity, and a deep sense of shared responsibility. Our team is rooted in purpose, driven by a growth mindset, and united by the desire to build something that truly matters.

Core Values

Humbly Confident - Trusted Teammate - Service Driven - Strategic Innovation -

Growth Mindset

Job Summary

The Fulfillment Inventory & Returns Specialist is responsible for maintaining accurate inventory records, processing product returns, and ensuring compliance with traceability and quality standards. This role serves as a critical link between fulfillment, inventory control, and quality assurance by managing discrepancies, returns, and product flow integrity.

Disclaimer:

This job description is intended to outline the core duties and qualifications of the role. It is not exhaustive and may be subject to change or revision. Employees may be required to perform other related duties as assigned, in support of the department or organization's goals, even if such tasks are not specifically listed in this document.

Key Responsibilities

5 Core Responsibilities:

  • Maintain Inventory Accuracy and Traceability
  • Process Returns, Corrections, and Product Reconciliation
  • Monitor and Resolve Inventory Discrepancies
  • Ensure Compliance with Inventory and Regulatory Standards
  • Support Cross-Functional Inventory Flow and Continuous Improvement

Key Duties & Expectations:

Inventory Management

  • Perform routine cycle counts and full inventory audits.
  • Maintain accurate inventory records across all systems (e.g., Cultivera, spreadsheets).
  • Reconcile physical inventory against digital records and resolve discrepancies.
  • Monitor inventory levels and flag shortages, overages, or anomalies.
  • Ensure proper bin placement, labeling, and storage standards are followed.

Returns & Corrections

  • Process returned orders, including inspection, documentation, and system adjustments.
  • Investigate root causes of returns (fulfillment error, damage, compliance issue, etc.).
  • Coordinate with QA and Fulfillment to determine product disposition (restock, rework, destroy).
  • Maintain detailed return logs and ensure proper documentation for compliance.
  • Execute rework or relabeling processes when required.

Compliance & Documentation

  • Ensure all inventory movements meet i502/WAC compliance standards.
  • Maintain audit-ready documentation for inventory adjustments and returns.
  • Assist with internal and external audits by providing accurate records.
  • Follow all SOPs related to inventory handling, traceability, and product control.

Cross-Functional Coordination

  • Work closely with Fulfillment, Packaging, QA, and Sales teams to resolve inventory issues.
  • Communicate discrepancies, delays, or risks to leadership in real time.
  • Support order packing on high volume days.

Continuous Improvement

  • Identify trends in returns and inventory errors and recommend process improvements.
  • Support Lean initiatives such as 5S, Standard Work, and root cause analysis.
  • Contribute to improving inventory workflows and system accuracy.

Key Duties & Expectations:

  • Leads, Manages, and holds Accountable (LMA) Fulfillment Leads and Assistants.
  • Supervises daily fulfillment operations and ensures timely, accurate order processing.
  • Assigns tasks and tracks progress using systems like S2S or Cultivera.
  • Coordinates with Packaging for product intake and order building.
  • Manages inventory, audits, and documentation (manifests, invoices, vendor communication).
  • Simplifies workflow for the team and provides real-time coaching.
  • Handles training, timesheets, and issue escalation.
  • Predicts workflow gaps and adjusts team scheduling and priorities accordingly.
  • Supervise daily order fulfillment tasks, including assigning duties and overseeing progress.

  • Actively contributes to process flow improvements by identifying inefficiencies, leading root cause analysis, and supporting cross-functional problem-solving.
  • Helps maintain steady, balanced workflows by minimizing downtime, flexing labor across tasks, and coordinating with upstream and downstream departments.

Qualifications

  • Excellent interpersonal skills, communication skills and self-motivation.

enrichment.score_rationale: The workflow is highly repeatable (inventory audits, returns processing, and reconciliation exist at virtually every fulfillment operation), but automation feasibility is mixed due to physical handling, compliance judgment calls, and cross-functional coordination requirements. The salary tops out at $25/hr, which is right at the threshold but the listed range starts at $18, keeping the anchor score at 0; only two niche tools (Cultivera, S2S) are mentioned, limiting the system specificity score, and phone/verbal communication with sales reps and leadership is explicitly noted.

description:
Love Catching Mistakes Before They Happen? This Role’s Built for You.

You notice the details others miss. You're the type who double-checks the math, confirms the product code, and makes sure it all adds up, because when it does, everything runs smoother. At Wisenbaker, we don’t just want that skill set, we depend on it.

As an Order Processing Specialist, you’ll be the engine behind clean, accurate, and on-time order releases. You’ll coordinate across teams, flag errors before they become issues, and make sure every project kicks off with clarity and precision. If you're a master of organization, quoting, and process flow, we’re ready to hand you the reins.

Your Mission:
You’ll own the quote-to-order process. From PO reconciliation and quoting to managing product orders and scheduling measures, you’ll be at the core of our order pipeline. It’s part detail wizard, part problem solver, and 100% essential.

What You’ll Be Doing:

Cross-reference account process sheets daily to stay current with updates
Process and release all product orders with total accuracy
Double-check data entry for order integrity and release sales orders
Handle quoting and PO reconciliation for internal orders — no loose ends
Schedule measures and manage customer order entries
Ensure product availability and lead-time expectations are met
Maintain and track the Need PO List
Provide excellent internal and external communication via phone and email
Attend model home and internal account meetings when applicable
Stay up to date with evolving product knowledge and builder systems
Cross-train on the Customer Service Rep role

What We’re Looking For:

High school diploma required — college degree preferred
1–2 years of customer service or administrative experience a plus
Sharp attention to detail with strong time management skills
Comfortable with basic math calculations and reconciling purchase orders
Strong written and verbal communication
Ability to work independently and under pressure
Proficient in Microsoft Office and Windows environments

About the Company

At Wisenbaker, we don’t just support the homebuilding industry, we help build it from the ground up. As one of the largest interior finish solutions providers in the country, we partner with top homebuilders to deliver flooring, countertops, and cabinets that turn houses into homes. But we’re more than materials, we’re a people-powered operation where hard work is noticed, growth is real, and leadership leads. If you're ready to be part of something built to last, welcome to Wisenbaker.

description:
Are you looking for an exciting opportunity working for a Global Technology Leader?

At Aviat Networks, we take great pride in hiring a workforce that is committed to supporting and strengthening our values and attributes. If you are a results-oriented, customer centric and innovative thinker who also takes pride in personal and professional integrity, Aviat Networks is the ideal next step in your career. We are looking for people who love to solve problems, enjoy change, and know how to have fun so come and join a dynamic team that strives to bring communications to the world.

Aviat Networks is the world’s largest independent supplier of wireless transmission systems. We are recognized worldwide for cutting-edge 5G, backhaul, networking solutions and services. Customers in more than 135 countries depend on Aviat Networks to build, expand, and upgrade their voice, data, and video solutions.

About the Role: The Order Fulfillment Planner will be responsible for ensuring that internal and external customer orders are processed, scheduled, monitored, and fulfilled accurately, promptly, and efficiently in support of meeting Aviat revenue goal(s). Interface with relevant Sales, Field Service Project Managers and/or Customers on order status, change orders, shipment tracking, delivery, and issue resolution. This is a role that will need a proactive person who is able to work on their own initiative to drive the orders through the system.

 Responsibilities:

Facilitates scheduling of orders and resolves issues (holds, shortages, etc.) on the order.
Manages promise dates in Oracle for revenue forecasting and update consistently to reflect ship date communicated to customer.
Coordinates with different departments (planning/purchasing/finance/warehouse) to ensure orders will ship per promise date.
Tracks and reports the cause of order processing delays.
Notifies Sales, PM, and/or Customer of issues in meeting customer expectations.
Maintains and executes Focus Customer specific tasks such as custom labeling requests, weekly reports, 3WM packings lists, 1 invoice per PO, etc.
Coordinates order changes post-booking with Network Design Engineers, Project Managers, and Order Management (i.e. part substitution, customer address change, additional equipment, etc.)
Reviews order status and ensures timely picks to meet on time delivery.
Transacts orders in Oracle through shipment process (includes, PO receipt, sales order pick and ship confirm).
Provides Sales and/or Customer with sales order confirmation and ship notifications.
Coordinates drop shipments of orders from multiple locations.
Responsible for selecting mode of transportation, communicating customer specific delivery requirements, and tracking the shipment from pick up through warehouse receipt to meet customer expectations.
Supplies shipment/equipment documentation to suppliers and customers.
Responsible for updating PO Approval list on order status and gaining approval before the order ships.
Issue license upgrades for all product lines.
Ensures orders are accepted by the customer and revenue is recognized.
Assists customers with physical inventory of orders or packing list receipt errors.
Acts as the Project Manager for equipment only orders including providing feedback to Project Analyst team upon project completion.
Participates in weekly Focus Customer calls or project kick-offs as Operations representative.
Facilitates customer returns (RMAs) for wrong or defective equipment and ensure replacement equipment is provided in a timely manner.
Must maintain the confidentiality of all customer and company information.
Other Duties as assigned

Qualifications & Competencies:

Bachelor’s degree or minimum 5 years’ experience in Operations.
Working knowledge/experience in Logistics is an advantage.
Knowledge of Sales, Project Management, Finance, Accounting and MRP strongly desired.
Proficient with Oracle ERP system and Excel/Word, strong analytical skills.
Ability to multitask, have strong organizational and time management skills are needed to ensure that the requested tasks are completed by the specific deadlines.
Candidates must be self-motivated and possess excellent oral and written communication skills including good telephone manners.
Proficient with Microsoft Office Suite, specifically excel – knowledge of pivot tables, vlookups, keyboard shortcuts, etc.
Excellent interpersonal and leadership skills are needed.
Strong Customer Service skills required.
Requires onsite support 2-3 days weekly, with daily onsite support in Austin, TX during each quarter end.

 We encourage you to read our Candidate Privacy Notice. You have the right to withdraw your consent at any time. To do this you can email us at privacy @aviatnet.com.

Aviat Networks provides equal employment opportunity for all applicants and employees. The Company does not discriminate against applicants or employees on the basis of race, color, sex, age, national origin, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, veteran or military status, disability or any other legally recognized protected basis under federal, state or local law.

Aviat Networks offers a competitive benefits package. Apply NOW to learn more!

enrichment.score_rationale: While the workflow is highly repeatable (order management roles exist at hundreds of companies) and partially rule-based, the role is heavily anchored on phone/call participation, judgment-based cross-departmental coordination, and customer-facing communication, limiting automation feasibility. Only one named API-capable tool (Oracle ERP) is mentioned, and salary is not disclosed, preventing a salary anchor score.

description:
Description:

Cottage Door Press is a young dynamic children’s publishing company located in Rolling Meadows, IL. We are currently in a rapid growth mode and are seeking to add an Order Management Coordinator to our operations and warehouse team based in Southeastern Wisconsin. This position will support the team by coordinating customer activities including orders, shipments, and invoicing. This person will work very closely with the Sales and Operations teams to manage the flow of goods to our customers.

Customer Communications/Maintenance:

Work with sales team to manage order requirements and respond in various order portals as needed
Maintain customer records and addresses in ERP system
Provide daily customer service support through Amazon Seller Central, including responding to customer inquiries, processing replacements, and issuing refunds
Gather and organize images for new Amazon Seller Central product listings, maintaining a structured shared drive
Assist with compliance management by locating required documentation and properly storing it in a shared drive
Support monthly project tracking by manipulating data and accurately entering it into a shared tracking sheet

Manage Order Flow:

Process incoming orders and manage the flow of future orders, back-order releases, etc.…
Support Amazon Advantage purchase orders for Lodi Publishing on a weekly basis
Ensure that Direct to Consumer orders are shipped in the required timeframe
Troubleshooting problems with orders, shipments, and deliveries

Assistant Liaison with 3PL Warehouse Fulfillment Center:

Ensure timely processing of orders by our 3PL Warehouse
Understand and communicate customer requirements to 3PL warehouse as outlined in customer routing guides

Accounts Receivables / Invoicing:

Assist in invoicing customers for shipments
Gathering documentation regarding credit claims

Inventory Analyst Responsibilities:

Assist with Amazon Seller Central inventory management, including coordinating and executing stock replenishments
Requirements:
Minimum of 3 yrs customer service experience or 2 yrs of college
Strong communications skills, written and oral
Organizational skills to manage large flow of information
Ability to multitask and prioritize projects
Proficiency in MS Excel
Proficiency with Google Apps and Gmail
Flexibility to function in a growing company

Optional Requirements:

Familiarity with EDI and the processing steps required to transact accordingly
Understanding of Retail customers and their unique requirements
Knowledge of Consumer Product Goods and books a strong plus.
Familiarity with ERP (Enterprise Resource Planning) solutions and Warehouse Management functions

description:
This is an on-site position in Brookfield, WI.

The Inventory / Order & Logistics Planner role is responsible for supporting customers through efficient order processing, product knowledge, and responsive communication while also overseeing inventory accuracy, stock levels, and material flow. This position serves as a key link between customers, operations, and the supply chain team to ensure seamless order fulfillment and exceptional service.

Duties and Responsibilities:

Serve as the primary internal point of contact for customer inquiries and orders.

Respond to customer requests regarding pricing, availability, lead times, and product specifications.

Update customer forecasts in ERP system weekly

Data entry into BI System for the Sales Department weekly.

Create, enter, and manage sales orders within the ERP system.

Maintain outbound shipment log daily

Communicate with customers prior to any delays and provide daily updates until resolution.

Maintain strong relationships with customers through professional and timely communication and follow up.

Coordinate with operations, purchasing, and logistics to ensure accurate and on-time deliveries.

Send weekly order report to production

Create weekly shipment plan for warehouse

Monitor inventory levels to ensure adequate stock and proactively address shortages.

Update and maintain departmental KPI’s.

Maintain product data, part numbers, and inventory records in the ERP system.

Create and email all customer invoices.

Provide forecast analytics for sales

Manage all customer prices and quotes in ERP and company drive.

description:
*Customer Service Representative*

Salary Range: $21.00 - $25.00

Shift: 8:30 AM - 5:00 PM

*Responsibilities: *

* Communicate with customers to receive and clarify job details, pricing, and timelines.
* Enter and manage job order tickets accurately in the production system.
* Work closely with sales, production, prepress, and mail data processing teams to coordinate each project.
* Monitor job progress and proactively update customers on status, changes, or issues.
* Review proofs, specs, and deadlines to ensure all details are correct before production.
* Handle customer questions or concerns promptly and professionally.
* Assist with order tracking, shipping coordination, and delivery confirmations as needed.
* Maintain organized records of orders, client communication, and project details.

*Qualifications: *

* Strong attention to detail and accuracy — especially when entering or reviewing job information.
* Excellent communication and multitasking skills.
* Ability to prioritize tasks and manage multiple projects under deadlines.
* Basic computer skills and comfort using spreadsheets, email, and databases.
* Printing experience is a plus, but we’re willing to train the right person who is eager to learn.
* Positive attitude, dependability, and a willingness to ask questions to fully understand each job.

Pay: $21.00 - $25.00 per hour

Benefits:
* 401(k)
* Health insurance
* Paid time off

Work Location: In person

positionName: Wine Picker and Packer Foklift operator
description:
Get into an entry level position in an exciting industry.

Start out as a wine picker and packer. Select wines from our inventory and pack in boxes to fill orders from the marketplace. We have a range of customers that need a mixed case of wine up to some that get many mixed and full cases of wine. You will put these orders together and sticker them with the customer order info that is printed out. Then send them out for loading and delivery.

Get to know the wines and other products in our portfolio. There are many positions and departments within the company for personal and professional growth.

Be a part of the dynamic beverage supply industry!

Job Types: Full-time, Part-time

Pay: From $18.00 per hour

Benefits:
* 401(k)
* Paid time off

Work Location: In person

description:
Description:

In this role, you will support and help lead customer service operations by accurately processing orders, resolving inquiries in a timely manner, and upholding our commitment to exceptional service. This position will initially support our Walmart account; however, responsibilities may evolve as the business grows. You will report directly to the Customer Service Manager.

What We Offer

Monday–Friday schedule
Hours: 7:00 AM – 3:30 PM
401(k) plan with company match
Annual performance reviews
Quarterly bonus opportunities
Comprehensive benefits package (medical, dental, vision, life insurance, and FSA) available after 30 days
Paid time off begins after 30 days
Employee discount on company products

Key Responsibilities

Manage and respond to incoming customer inquiries professionally, ensuring all issues are addressed within 24 hours.
Accurately enter customer purchase orders into SAP within 24 hours of receipt.
Review all purchase orders to ensure complete accuracy in data entry.
Ensure on-time or early delivery of all customer orders.
Provide high-quality support across billing, order management, and issue resolution.
Gather and analyze customer information to assess needs, resolve issues, and reduce repeat inquiries.
Share relevant customer insights and trends with the sales team.
Prepare and distribute customer-related reports as needed.
Coordinate with internal departments to set up new customer accounts promptly.
Process customer returns within 48 hours to ensure prompt credit issuance.
Collaborate effectively with internal teams as well as Walmart and Sam’s Club partners.
Analyze inventory and sales data to drive optimal on-time and in-full (OTIF) performance.
Requirements:
5+ years of experience in a customer service or customer service administration role
Demonstrated leadership ability
Strong analytical skills with the ability to interpret data and communicate insights
close attention to detail and accuracy
Ability to thrive in a fast-paced environment
Excellent time management and organizational skills

Preferred Qualifications

Experience with SAP, Microsoft Outlook, Excel, and Word
Familiarity with Walmart systems (e.g., Scintilla, Supplier One) is a plus
Prior experience in food manufacturing strongly preferred
Experience working with Walmart is a plus

Physical & Mental Demands

This role requires prolonged sitting, frequent use of hands, and clear communication. Occasional standing, walking, stooping, or reaching may be required. Vision requirements include close vision, distance vision, color vision, peripheral vision, depth perception, and the ability to adjust focus.

The position also requires strong problem-solving and critical thinking skills, with the ability to manage multiple priorities and meet deadlines in a fast-paced environment. The individual must demonstrate a high level of attention to detail, frequent decision-making, and the ability to analyze data and interpret trends. Success in this role depends on maintaining focus, adaptability, and responsiveness while handling a high volume of tasks and shifting priorities.

Work Environment

Work is performed in an indoor environment with protection from weather conditions; however, temperatures may vary.

description:
Hamilton Beach Brands is a leading designer, marketer, and distributor of innovative household and commercial appliances trusted by consumers around the world. Founded in 1910 and headquartered in Glen Allen, Virginia, we develop and market a broad portfolio of products under well-known brands including Hamilton Beach®, Weston®, Lotus®, and Hamilton Beach Professional®.

For more than a century, our products have helped simplify everyday life – from preparing meals and beverages at home to supporting foodservice professionals in restaurants, hotels, and commercial kitchens.

Our culture is guided by a shared set of values that shape how we work and make decisions every day: Customer Obsession, People, Good Thinking, Ethics, Passion for Quality, Embrace Change, Teamwork, and commitment to Our Environment and Community.

At Hamilton Beach Brands, we look for people who are collaborative, curious, and motivated to make meaningful impact.

About the Role

The position has the main objective of processing customers’ orders in the division ensuring timely and accurate completion from entry to customer’s receipt in order to consistently meet or exceed customer’s expectations. The position must anticipate product availability challenges from both US and China product sourcing by building and maintaining efficient communication network with team members involved in most of HBB locations while responding directly to customers demands. This position is the most responsible for measuring and driving best-in-class customer service in the division.

This is a hybrid position based in Miami, Florida, and will report to Director, Sales- LATAM.

What You’ll Do

• Manage customer orders end‑to‑end, including entry, modifications, inventory vetting, order drops, and full fulfillment follow‑through.

• Monitor product availability, anticipate shortages, and coordinate actions to resolve back‑order and supply issues across the division.

• Oversee open orders through delivery, maintaining consistent communication with warehouses, distribution teams, and customers to ensure accurate, on‑time shipments.

• Maintain updated country‑level forecasts aligned with customer needs and business requirements.

• Track DI order status, support customer documentation needs, and ensure accuracy between DI invoicing and actual shipments to prevent discrepancies.

• Coordinate required customer documentation, including Certificates of Origin, labeling compliance (Ecuador), and VASR job creation for PriceSmart and Sam’s Club.

• Support claims, invoicing, pricing maintenance, and reporting, including price adjustments, monthly/quarterly customer reports, and FTZ documentation.

• Provide administrative and operational support such as call handling, Logility monitoring, PowerPoint creation, and maintaining client pricing lists.

What We’re Looking For

• College degree or equivalent experience.

• A minimum of 5 years’ experience in demand planning and customer operations, with a focus on sales and distribution in the region, is preferred.

• Ability to integrate, coordinate and drive inter-disciplinary teams’ members to perform successfully.

• Experience in the durable goods industry with shipments into Latin America is preferred.

• Bilingual English Spanish is required.

• Proficiency in Excel is required.

• Experience in customer service/operations/demand/planning with customers common to HBB is preferred.

This description reflects general physical and environmental requirements necessary to perform the essential functions of the position. Reasonable accommodation may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform these functions.

What We Offer

Hamilton Beach Brands offers a competitive compensation and benefits package designed to support your well-being and long-term success. Our offerings include:

Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision coverage
Company HSA contributions
401(k) plan with immediate vesting
Profit sharing program
Paid vacation, holidays, and a paid charitable day
Summer hours to support work-life balance
Employee discounts on Hamilton Beach products

Applicants must be authorized to work for ANY employer in the U.S. We are unable to sponsor or take over sponsorship of an employment visa at this time.

Our employees enjoy working in a tobacco-free and drug-free environment. We do not consider candidates who use tobacco products.

Hamilton Beach Brands, Inc. is committed to hiring and retaining a diverse workforce. We are an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer, making decisions without regard to race, color, religion, creed, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, national origin, age, veteran status, disability, or any other protected class. Our company uses E-Verify to confirm the employment eligibility of all newly hired employees. To learn more about E-Verify, including your rights and responsibilities, please visit www.E-Verify.gov.

#LI-HYBRID

description:
*Company Overview*
Leach Camper Sales of Lincoln is a locally owned dealership in Lincoln, Nebraska and have been since 1963. We specialize in motorhomes and towable campers. We pride ourselves on employees that have been with us from 5 years to over 40 years.

*Job Summary*
We are seeking a detail-oriented Purchasing Specialist to join our team! In this vital role, you will drive the procurement process, ensuring the timely acquisition of quality inventory and supplies. Your expertise will help optimize our supply chain operations, manage vendor relationships, and support our continued growth in the recreational vehicle industry. Duties include but not limited to:

* Manage the end-to-end purchasing process using our DMS and online platforms of manufactures and specialized parts vendors.
* Source products efficiently by evaluating suppliers, negotiating prices, and securing favorable contracts to maximize value.
* Coordinate with coworkers to maintain optimal stock levels, prevent shortages, and control inventory costs.
* Collaborate with logistics providers to streamline delivery schedules and ensure timely receipt of goods.
* Maintain comprehensive records of purchase orders, contracts, and supplier communications to ensure transparency and compliance.
* Work closely with service technicians and writers
* Monitor DMS and maintain ETA status to Service Writers & customers
* Provide excellent customer service
* Build trusted relationships with our customers to assist them on quality selection of parts and accessories

*Requirements Preferred*

* Strong knowledge of materials management, inventory control, procurement processes, and supply chain management principles.
* Demonstrated ability to negotiate effectively with vendors to secure competitive pricing and favorable terms.
* Personality to have the ability to interface with multiple people at different stages in the procurement process
* Excellent organizational skills with attention to detail in managing purchase documentation and supplier relationships.
* Prior experience in the recreational vehicle or automotive industry is a plus but not required.

*Join us as a Purchasing Specialist and be part of a vibrant team dedicated to excellence in every aspect of our business! Your expertise will help us deliver quality parts & accessories to our great customers.*

Pay: From $20.00 per hour

Benefits:
* 401(k)
* 401(k) matching
* Dental insurance
* Health insurance
* Health savings account
* Life insurance
* Paid time off
* Vision insurance

Work Location: In person
descriptionHTML:
Company Overview
Leach Camper Sales of Lincoln is a locally owned dealership in Lincoln, Nebraska and have been since 1963. We specialize in motorhomes and towable campers. We pride ourselves on employees that have been with us from 5 years to over 40 years.

Job Summary
We are seeking a detail-oriented Purchasing Specialist to join our team! In this vital role, you will drive the procurement process, ensuring the timely acquisition of quality inventory and supplies. Your expertise will help optimize our supply chain operations, manage vendor relationships, and support our continued growth in the recreational vehicle industry. Duties include but not limited to:

  • Manage the end-to-end purchasing process using our DMS and online platforms of manufactures and specialized parts vendors.
  • Source products efficiently by evaluating suppliers, negotiating prices, and securing favorable contracts to maximize value.
  • Coordinate with coworkers to maintain optimal stock levels, prevent shortages, and control inventory costs.
  • Collaborate with logistics providers to streamline delivery schedules and ensure timely receipt of goods.
  • Maintain comprehensive records of purchase orders, contracts, and supplier communications to ensure transparency and compliance.
  • Work closely with service technicians and writers
  • Monitor DMS and maintain ETA status to Service Writers & customers
  • Provide excellent customer service
  • Build trusted relationships with our customers to assist them on quality selection of parts and accessories

Requirements Preferred

  • Strong knowledge of materials management, inventory control, procurement processes, and supply chain management principles.
  • Demonstrated ability to negotiate effectively with vendors to secure competitive pricing and favorable terms.
  • Personality to have the ability to interface with multiple people at different stages in the procurement process
  • Excellent organizational skills with attention to detail in managing purchase documentation and supplier relationships.
  • Prior experience in the recreational vehicle or automotive industry is a plus but not required.

Join us as a Purchasing Specialist and be part of a vibrant team dedicated to excellence in every aspect of our business! Your expertise will help us deliver quality parts & accessories to our great customers.

Pay: From $20.00 per hour

Benefits:

  • 401(k)
  • 401(k) matching
  • Dental insurance
  • Health insurance
  • Health savings account
  • Life insurance
  • Paid time off
  • Vision insurance

Work Location: In person

enrichment.score_rationale: The purchasing/procurement workflow is highly repeatable and exists across countless dealerships, but the role mixes automatable tasks (PO management, ETA tracking) with judgment-heavy ones (vendor negotiation, customer-facing parts advising) and customer service interactions that imply some phone/counter duties. The DMS tool is mentioned but not named, limiting system specificity, and pay at $20/hr falls below the $25/hr salary anchor threshold.

Source products by evaluating suppliers and negotiating prices
Coordinate with coworkers to maintain optimal stock levels and control inventory costs
Collaborate with logistics providers to streamline delivery schedules
Maintain records of purchase orders, contracts, and supplier communications

Work closely with service technicians and service writers
Provide customer service and assist customers with parts and accessories selection
Build relationships with customers and vendors

description:
\*\*\* This is a Temporary opportunity with the possibility of hire and/or extension \*\*\*

*Location*: Madison, WI

*Pay*: $35 - 38/ hr.

MilliporeSigma is the North American life science brand of Merck KGaA, Darmstadt, Germany – a leading science and technology company. It offers a broad range of innovative products and services to biotech and pharmaceutical drug therapies businesses. Through dedicated collaboration with the scientific and engineering communities, and as one of the top three R&D investors in the life science tools industry, the brand serves as a strategic partner to customers and helps advance the promise of life science.

Position Summary

The Procurement Specialist position is a senior-level technical position within the supply chain function and fulfillment job family. This position is responsible for the first point of entry into the procurement organization and expected to quickly understand the operations and impact procurement has on other up and downstream departments.

This role ensures that materials are obtained in a timely manner and from suppliers that meet or exceed or quality requirements. Transactional and strategic work includes but is not limited to obtaining/maintaining prices and delivery information from the suppliers. Ensuring purchase orders and material master data is updated and accurate. Working with suppliers and other internal teams to ensure timely delivery and release of materials to meet production schedules. Host and attend internal and external meetings to ensure alignment with delivery timelines, identify risks and alternative supply options.

*Minimum Requirements*

* 4+ years of experience in a manufacturing environment with direct purchasing requirements
* 3+ Procurement or purchasing function using an MRP and/or ERP systems.
* 4+ years experience analyzing data using SAP and Microsoft Office

*Preferred Requirements:*

* Certification ASCM CPIM, CSCP or equivalent
* Familiarity with Lean or Six Sigma methodologies
* Strong analytical abilities with problem solving techniques, unconditional commitment to teamwork and communication.
* Ability to follow ISO and cGMP standards and utilize quality management systems.
* Ability to drive initiatives with cross functional teams and stakeholders.
* Ability to communicate at different levels of the business
* High sense of urgency and results-oriented
* Extremely organized and detail oriented
* Apparent Negotiation skills
* Excellent communication and interpersonal abilities.
* Ability to work independently and as part of a team.
* Proficient in Excel (pivot tables, vlookup, IF)

*Job Specifics Skills: *

* Develop and maintain purchasing schedules, ensuring alignment with production schedule, timelines and milestones.
* Collaborate with schedulers, suppliers, and new product introduction teams to gather necessary information to drive on time purchasing and delivery of materials.
* Monitor schedule, orders and project progress and adjust to accommodate changes or delays to ensure on-time delivery.
* Work closely with supplier quality and quality control to ensure timely set up and release of materials to support on-time production starts.
* Identify potential procurement or purchasing challenges and propose and implement solutions to mitigate risk.
* Communicate effectively with stakeholders regarding procurement updates and changes.
* Assist to identify vendors that will meet the quality, safety, and financial requirements of the site and implement as an approved source of supply
* Review and evaluate procured materials to ensure sufficient stock while maintaining inventory goals and overall schedule adherence. Develop and deliver forecasts as required.
* Review and maintain critical procurement master data to drive procurement processes including but not limited to source list, info records, purchase requestions/orders, and sales orders.
* Support and drive sourcing efforts at the site level including but not limited to obtaining information from internal teams or driving trials of sourced materials.
* Support training and operating procedure development and updates.
* Utilize quality management systems to document changes, deviations and corrections.
* Host and attend meetings with internal and external teams to drive delivery and provide clarity on status of purchasing activities.
* Anticipate procurement risks as the result of onboarding new projects and work to mitigate.
* Miscellaneous duties and tasks assigned.

This Company is an Equal Employment Opportunity employer. No employee or applicant for employment will be discriminated against on the basis of race, color, religion, age, sex, sexual orientation, national origin, ancestry, disability, military or veteran status, genetic information, gender identity, transgender status, marital status, or any other classification protected by applicable federal, state, or local law. This policy of Equal Employment Opportunity applies to all policies and programs relating to recruitment and hiring, promotion, compensation, benefits, discipline, termination, and all other terms and conditions of employment. Any applicant or employee who believes they have been discriminated against by the Company or anyone acting on behalf of the Company must report any concerns to his/her Human Resources Business Partner, Legal, or Compliance immediately. The Company will not retaliate against any individual because he/she made a good faith report of discrimination.

Job Types: Full-time, Temp-to-hire

Pay: $35.00 - $38.00 per hour



Education:
* Bachelor's (Preferred)

Experience:
* SAP ERP: 1 year (Preferred)
* Demand planning: 1 year (Preferred)
* Supply chain: 1 year (Preferred)

Work Location: In person

description:
Job Summary:

Under direction of the Central Processing Manager, the Central Processing Support I will be responsible for the daily processing and monitoring of the Bank’s Central Processing Support functions. Responsibilities include balance DDA work in progress, GL and internal bank accounts, process and research wire transfers, balance cash value, process cash orders, large item reviews, returned item and NSF processing, ACH origination transactions and assist with ATM/Debit Cards. This position will also help with the legal processing of levies, and subpoenas.

Essential Functions:

Responsible for performing all back-office operations and will work closely with the Central Processing Supervisor. Responsible for assisting the Central Processing Supervisor control and manage operations, as well as take a proactive approach towards problem resolution.
Serve as a resource and contact to bank personnel, clients, and vendors in all areas of central processing support.
Daily functions may include:
Review and scrutinize daily reports
Balance and maintain DDA Work In Progress, general ledger accounts and internal bank accounts
Assist in clearing and managing PCBB out of balance conditions
Process incoming and outgoing wire transfers
Wire research, daily audit & balancing of wire system
Perform timely and accurate check clearing adjustments with FRB
Process cash orders and balance cash vault
Balancing of Money Gram (Cashier’s Checks)
Large item review of signatures and endorsements
Process of deposited returned items and NSF Items
Exception Processing balancing and clearing
Process ACH items and perform maintenance
Process ACH origination transactions
Process MasterCard ATM/Debit Card disputes
Facilitate the return of funds for fraudulent transactions
Process new accounts and scan new accounts signatures for Finance related accounts
Maintain proper rates and interest payments for institutional or brokered Certificates of Deposit accounts
Assist in scanning and shredding old documents
Process levies and attachments
Research and assist with subpoena research
Process SSA/SSI administration requests
Assist Associates with questions and inquiries
Assist the Finance Department in research and reconciling of correspondent bank and FRB accounts to the general ledger.
Assist Operations Management with Certifications and timely completion and reporting of Currency Transaction Reports.
Perform other duties and special projects as needed and assigned.
A minimum of 1-2 years banking experience with emphasis in operations
Require the ability to be flexible and work independently
Able to fill in for any position within the Central Processing Support department.
The ability to cross sell the Bank’s products and services to existing clients as well as prospects, growing new profitable relationships by referring opportunities to other parts of the company.
Good written and verbal communication skills
Good phone etiquette
Team player and self-starter
Ability to manage multiple tasks and meet deadlines
Good analytical and research skills
Present professional image and work well in a team environment.

description:
*About Us*

Headquartered in the dynamic city of *Houston, Texas*, *Smart Supply Chain* is a premier freight forwarding company with a truly *global footprint*. Backed by a powerful network of *over 200 international agents*, we deliver seamless, reliable, and innovative logistics solutions across the globe.

What sets us apart is our unwavering commitment to *innovation*, *sustainable growth*, and building *long-term partnerships*. We don't just move freight—we move businesses forward by redefining what excellence in supply chain management looks like.

*About the Role*

We are seeking a detail-oriented *Shipping Coordinator* with strong experience in *LTL (Less-Than-Truckload)* freight to manage outbound and inbound shipments. This role is responsible for coordinating carriers, preparing shipping documentation, tracking freight, and ensuring timely, cost-effective deliveries.

*Wage Rate*

Starting $50,000.00

*Responsibilities *

* Coordinate and schedule *LTL shipments* with approved carriers
* Prepare and verify bills of lading (BOLs), shipping labels, and related documentation
* Track shipments, monitor delivery status, and proactively resolve delays or issues
* Communicate with carriers, warehouses, customer service, and internal teams
* Audit freight invoices and address discrepancies
* Optimize shipping routes and carrier selection for cost and efficiency
* Maintain accurate shipping records and reports
* Ensure compliance with company policies and transportation regulations
* Negotiate rates with carriers

*Experience & Skills*

* 4+ years of experience in *shipping, logistics, or transportation*, with strong LTL exposure
* Knowledge of freight classifications, NMFC codes, and carrier pricing
* Experience working with TMS, ERP, or shipping software
* Strong organizational skills and attention to detail
* Excellent communication and problem-solving abilities
* Ability to manage multiple shipments and priorities in a fast-paced environment
* Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and basic reporting tools

*Preferred Qualifications*

* Experience negotiating carrier rates
* Background in manufacturing, distribution, or warehouse environments
* Familiarity with freight claims and dispute resolution

*Work Schedule:*

* Full-time; 8:00am – 5:00pm

*What We Offer*

* Competitive salary ($50k–$60k)
* Health, dental, and vision insurance
* Paid time off and holidays
* Growth opportunities within logistics and supply chain operations

Job Type: Full-time

Pay: From $50,000.00 per year



Experience:
* LTL : 1 year (Required)
* Shipping Coordinator : 1 year (Required)
* Negotiation Rates with Carriers: 1 year (Required)

Language:
* Spanish (Preferred)
* Mandarin (Preferred)

Work Location: In person

description:
OVERVIEW

As an eCommerce Fulfillment Specialist Professional , you will play a crucial role in ensuring efficient order processing, assembly of parts, and shipment for our online retail operations. Utilizing the various computer shipping programs, the fulfillment specialist will ensure that orders are correct and being shipped out in a timely fashion. The fulfillment specialist will operate in a multi-faceted role. The fulfillment specialist will operate on the USPS & UPS shipping smaller products and packages. No Forklift is required for this position. You will be responsible for picking, packing, assembling, and shipping customer orders accurately and timely. Must have strong attention to detail, excellent organizational skills, and a commitment to meeting quality and productivity standards in a fast-paced environment and ability to work independently . The fulfillment specialist must have excellent computer skill and will operate various computer programs to complete product orders, print shipping labels and ensure timely delivery of product. The success of this role requires critical thinking, problem-solving, and organization skills. This includes filling store orders in an accurate and safe manner while monitoring and ensuring product accuracy and quality. This position requires someone who can maintain a fast, steady pace with strong attention to accuracy. No drug use or Felonies and be able to pass drug test and Background test.

RESPONSIBILITIES

* Verify customer information to ensure the package is sent to and received by the correct customer.
* Perform quality control checks to verify the accuracy and condition of products before shipment.
* Assembling Parts for shipping.
* Use order management systems and shipping software to process orders, generate shipping labels, and update order status.
* Responsible for ensuring that warehouse merchandise is stocked correctly, in proper order, and maintain organization and tidiness of shelves and racks.
* Clean and organize shipping and processing areas to ensure a safe working area.

REQUIREMENTS

* High school diploma or equivalent.
* Must be at least 18 years of age.
* Must be able to frequently lift at least 25lbs.
* Must be able to stand and walk for 4 hours per day and safely use ladders and step stools consistently throughout the workday.
* Ability to multitask, prioritize, and manage time effectively.
* Detail-oriented with a focus on accuracy and efficiency.
* Ability to work independently as well as part of a team.
* Must be extremely detail-oriented
* Excellent computer knowledge, proficient in Microsoft Office and Google Docs; must learn and become proficient in Quickbooks, Shipstation and UPS software.
* Regular and reliable attendance.
* Must Pass Background & drug test - Random Drug Testing
* No Felonies or domestic violence

FULL TIME POSITION 8AM -5PM PAY $18-$26 AN HOUR

Pay: $18.00 - $26.00 per hour

Work Location: In person
