descriptionHTML:
Drive Sales, Support Success, and Be the Backbone of Our Team!

At Dillon Toyota Lift Warehouse Solutions, we know that behind every great sales team is an even greater support system. That’s where you come in! We’re looking for a detail-oriented and customer-focused individual to keep our Warehouse Solutions team running smoothly.

This is more than just order processing – it’s about creating a seamless experience for our customers and sales team. You’ll be the go-to person for tracking orders, managing invoices, coordinating shipments, and ensuring every sale meets our high standards for quality and service.

You’ll work together with our sales staff, keeping things organized and efficient while maintaining strong attention to profitability, accuracy, and customer satisfaction. Dillon Toyota Lift Warehouse Solutions is a reputable company where you will be valued and supported in a positive, family-oriented environment. Join our team in Salt Lake City today!

What We Offer for Sales Support Administrator:
  • Financial Security:
    • Highly Competitive Salary
    • 401K Retirement Plan
    • Paid Time Off & Holiday Pay
    • Company Laptop Provided
  • Health & Wellness:
    • Excellent Medical, Dental, and Vision Plans –Starting 1st of the month following hire!
    • Flexible Spending Account & Health Saving Account Plans
    • Company Paid Life Insurance
    • Supplemental Benefits: Injury, Disability, & Critical Illness
  • Company Culture
    • Fun Company Events
    • Supportive, Family-Oriented Work Environment
    • Company that Values, Respects, and Listens to its Employees
    • Career Development Opportunities
Your Impact as Sales Support Administrator:
  • Provide exceptional customer service receiving inbound customer calls, emails, walk-in inquiries, and in-person visits – answering questions, discussing solutions, processing orders and follow up with sales teams as needed
  • Closely work with sales staff to ensure a seamless customer service approach.
  • Prepare quotes, proposals, and order documentation in collaboration with the outside sales team.
  • Coordinate with suppliers, vendors, and internal departments (engineering, operations, logistics) to ensure product availability and delivery timelines.
  • Maintain and update systems with customer data, sales activity, and order tracking.
  • Follow up on provided quotes to drive conversions, assist in finalizing sales, and ensure timely closure of orders and invoices.
  • Assist with inventory tracking, pricing updates, and documentation related to material handling equipment
  • Other duties as assigned
What You Bring to the Table as Sales Support Administrator:
  • High school diploma. Bachelor’s degree and/or equivalent work experience preferred
  • Experience in inside sales, customer service, or sales coordination, preferably in warehouse solutions, industrial equipment, or a similar industry is a plus
  • Familiarity with material handling equipment (e.g., racking systems, conveyors, forklifts, automation solutions) is a strong asset
  • Be able to pass a background check & drug screening

Who We Are at Dillon Toyota Lift:
Starting as a small, family-owned forklift dealership, Dillon Toyota Lift has grown into a full-service dealership with four locations throughout Idaho and Utah. We offer multiple product lines, racking, parts, service, rentals, warehouse solutions, and energy solutions, offering solutions to material handling needs! Dillon Toyota Lift has warehouse solutions that include design and installation, racking, mezzanines, dock equipment, conveyors, safety equipment, and a dedicated Warehouse Solutions team. Dillon Toyota Lift is a company that redefines what it means to consistently maintain a great company culture. Join our dynamic team today!

Dillon Toyota Lift offers a family-oriented culture, competitive salary, and comprehensive benefits package that includes health insurance, paid time off, 401(k) retirement plan and career development opportunities. Dillon Toyota Lift is an Equal Opportunity Employer

description:
*Relli Technology Inc.*

* *Location*: Duluth, GA
* *Job Type*: Full Time
* *Title*: Data Entry Position
* *Pay*: $21.00 per hour
* *Career Level*: Prefer experienced, but not required
* *Education*: High School diploma or equivalent and above
* *Category*: Aerospace / Aviation / Defense

*Job Description:*

Relli Technology is currently recruiting an *data entry *for our Asian Division. If you are a fast learner, dedicated worker, and looking for a company that offers opportunities for growth, this position may be right for you. The role will be based in Duluth, GA. Responsibilities include:

* Processing data entry of customer purchase orders into the Relli system
* Preparing worksheets for quoting and purchase order reviews
* Submitting RFQs and purchase orders to suppliers
* Entering supplier quotes into the Relli system
* Following up on order acknowledgments and late deliveries
* Verifying inputs from internal suppliers and outputs before delivery to internal customers
* Communicating with vendors

*Preferred Qualifications:*

* Knowledge or experience in the aerospace, aviation, or defense industry preferred
* Proficiency in MS Office (Word, Excel, Outlook)
* Excellent organizational skills
* Strong verbal and written communication skills in both English and Korean
* Ability to work effectively in a team environment
* Local candidates preferred

This is a great opportunity for someone who enjoys challenges, wants to learn about an exciting industry, and is seeking a rewarding career. Relli Technology offers *competitive compensation*, an *excellent 401(k) plan*, and *comprehensive medical, dental, life, and disability insurance*.

Please send your resume to *hr@relli.com* for consideration.

Job Type: Full-time

Pay: From $21.00 per hour

Benefits:
* 401(k)
* Dental insurance
* Health insurance
* Paid time off
* Vision insurance



Ability to Commute:
* Duluth, GA 30099 (Required)

Ability to Relocate:
* Duluth, GA 30099: Relocate before starting work (Preferred)

Work Location: In person

description:
OSG USA, INC. is a leading manufacturer of taps, endmills, drills, and indexable cutting tools. OSG also serves the fastener industry by offering a complete line of thread rolling, flat, cylindrical, and planetary, rack and trim dies. The company markets its products to numerous industries including automotive, die mold, aerospace, energy, heavy industry and dental.

Position Summary:

OSG USA, INC. is seeking an experienced and customer-focused Inventory Control Coordinator I to join our growing team. The Inventory Control Coordinator I plays a critical role in the organization processing orders and reorders as required to monitor and maintain inventory control records and levels. Expedites existing past due orders with factories and suppliers.

Job Functions for a Inventory Control Coordinator I:
Enters new purchase orders and transfer orders as required.
Runs Master Planning to create production orders for coating jobs and private label orders
Ensures that the facility has proper paperwork to receive and process goods.
Performs activities to expedite stock inventory with factories.
Assist in house account order processing and reporting.
Receives purchase orders and transfer orders from vendors. Reconciles actual quantities against ordered quantites and resolves any merchandising problems that may inhibit the receipt and/or shipment of goods.
Assists in resolving invoice variances
Runs reports for data analysis by planners
Other duties as assigned

Minimum Qualifications for a Inventory Control Coordinator I:
High School Diploma, G.E.D, or equivalent
General knowledge of business
Basic knowledge of Microsoft Office including Word and Excel

Preferred Qualifications for a Inventory Control Coordinator I:
Previous experience with a ERP service

Benefits: Excellent, Low-Cost Benefits
401K Tax Deferral & Profit-Sharing Plan with full employer match up to 6%
Comprehensive Premium PPO medical plan, dental and vision coverage
Wellness Program Incentive - earn a discount on your medical premiums
Flexible Savings Account (for medical and dependent care)
Company-paid Employee Basic Life/Dependent Life Insurance
Company paid Short Term and Term Disability Insurance
Begin accruing vacation immediately
Up to 15 PTO days in your first year
Up to 3 Flex days each year
10 paid holidays
Tuition reimbursement program
Employee Assistance Program

Physical Working Demands:
To perform the duties of this job, an individual must be able to read, write, understand and communicate by telephone using the English Language.
While performing the duties of this job, the employee is regularly required to sit for long periods of time (3-4 hours at a time). The employee is frequently required to use hands for typing, 10-key operation, telephone dialing, feeling objects, tools, or controls. Must be able to talk and hear.
Specific vision abilities required to this job include close vision, distance vision, peripheral vision, depth perception, and the ability to adjust focus. The employee is regularly required to enter information into a computer.

Compensation Type:
$19-$22 hour depending on experience + monthly bonus potential

Reports to: Inventory Control Manager
Job Type: Full-time, Non-Exempt
Hours: 8 a.m. - 5 p.m. Monday - Friday, hybrid work schedule
Location: OSG USA, Inc., 620 Stetson Avenue, St. Charles, IL 60174

We welcome the opportunity to speak with you about becoming part of our team. Learn more about OSG USA, Inc. at www.osgtool.com.

description:
*Job Overview*
We are seeking a motivated and detail-oriented Data Entry Associate to join our dynamic team. In this role, you will be responsible for accurately inputting, updating, and maintaining data, ensuring the integrity and accessibility of information. Your energetic approach and organizational skills will help streamline data processes, support operational efficiency, and contribute to the overall success of our organization. This paid position offers an exciting opportunity to develop your clerical and administrative expertise in a fast-paced environment.

*Duties*

* TYPING
* Enter description and photos onto our auction website
* Must also be familiar with Microsoft Excel spreadsheets with precision and speed
* Verify accuracy of data by cross-referencing source documents and performing quality checks
* Organize and file physical and digital records systematically for easy retrieval
* Have the ability to work efficiently to ensure all listings are entered and active on a very strict timeline

*Requirements*

* TYPING PROFICIENCY AND SPEED
* Proven experience with data entry, clerical tasks, or administrative support in an office environment
* Strong computer skills, including proficiency in Microsoft Excel and Access
* Excellent typing speed with high accuracy; capable of handling large volumes of data efficiently
* Prior office experience involving data entry, transcription, or order processing is preferred
* Demonstrated organizational skills to manage multiple tasks simultaneously and meet strict deadlines

Join us as a Data Entry Associate to play a vital role in maintaining accurate records that drive our operations forward. Your dedication to detail and enthusiasm for efficient workflows will make a meaningful impact on our team’s success!

Pay: $15.00 - $17.00 per hour

Benefits:
* 401(k) matching
* Flexible schedule
* Paid time off

Work Location: In person

Use Microsoft Excel spreadsheets for data management

description:
Summary:

The Automation Installation Coordinator provides critical administrative support to the installation team to ensure timely and successful equipment deliveries to customers. This role works closely with managers and supervisors within the group, while collaborating with the purchasing team, inside sales, vendors, and customers. Key responsibilities include managing pre-installation coordination, document review, spreadsheet and schedule maintenance, report compilation, information distribution, daily team check-ins, and communication support.

Essential Duties and Responsibilities:

Create new sales orders and packets.
Send welcome emails to new customers.
Assists order processing for phone/fax/email orders and provides assistance for online ordering.
Documenting and following up on important actions given to them from meetings.
Preparing necessary presentation materials for meetings.
Ensure all installation paperwork is together with service reports from technicians.
Contacting customers to set up conference calls for pre-installs or exit calls.
Updating internal spreadsheets, schedules, backorder parts and work orders.
Conducts daily check-ins with the installation teams and updates charts and schedules.

Additional Duties:

Performs other responsibilities as assigned based on organizational needs.

Qualifications:

To perform this job successfully, and individual must be team oriented and possess excellent verbal and written communication skills, strong multitasking abilities, and high attention to detail. The candidate must be able to clearly document and communicate ideas, strategies, problems, and solutions in both verbal and written forms.

Education and/or Experience:

A high school degree is required for this role.
A 2-year degree and/or 3 years of customer service are preferred.
Familiarity with Microsoft AX, Word, PowerPoint, Excel, Salesforce, CAD, Smart Sheet and/or ADP.

description:
Who we are:

MMI is a plastic injection molding and engineering solutions provider headquartered in Saline, Michigan, with offices in Troy, Warren, and Monterrey, Mexico. We specialize in delivering engineering solutions for advanced composites and engineered resins across diverse industries.

Our mission is to Deliver a Quality Product, On Time, at a Competitive Price.

We are committed to fostering an inclusive, welcoming workplace where innovation thrives and collaboration is at the heart of everything we do.

MMI Engineered Solutions is currently seeking a detail-oriented and reliable Customer Service Representative to join our Operations team. This role is central to ensuring excellent service and on-time delivery for our customers. You will serve as the primary point of contact, working cross-functionally with production, scheduling, sales, and engineering to support customer satisfaction and operational excellence.

Key Responsibilities:

Act as the main contact for assigned customer accounts, managing all communication and support needs

Coordinate and communicate changes to orders or delivery timelines with customers and internal stakeholders.
Understand and communicate customer-specific requirements across the organization (packaging, shipping, labeling, ordering, etc.).

Manage customer sales orders in the ERP system, ensuring data accuracy and timely processing.

Review and resolve daily EDI order issues; monitor and maintain customer portals as needed.

Maintain communication with the production scheduler to align delivery schedules and production priorities.

Monitor finished goods inventory and recommend adjustments to support customer demand.

Track and report on key metrics such as on-time delivery, backlog status, and performance trends.

Support product launches and the setup of new items, shipping locations, and routing requirements.

Assist with the development of order processing procedures and contingency plans.

Support continuous improvement efforts by identifying service gaps and proposing solutions.

Required Qualifications:

Bachelor's degree in Business Administration, Supply Chain, or a related field or 5+ years of relevant experience.

Strong background in customer service, preferably in manufacturing or the automotive industry.

Proactive mindset with a focus on problem-solving and customer satisfaction.
Excellent communication skills-both written and verbal.
Detail-oriented with strong organizational and multitasking abilities.

Familiarity with ERP systems, EDI processing, and sales order management.

Experience with OEM portals (e.g., Ford, GM, Stellantis, Mack/Volvo) is a plus.

Knowledge of EDI standards (X12 and EDIFACT) preferred.

Work EnvironmentExpectations:

Office-based role with occasional time spent in the manufacturing environment.

Minimal physical demands; primarily desk/clerical work.

Stop any shipment if customer requirements or quality concerns arise.
Maintain high levels of customer satisfaction and operational accuracy.
Why work at MMI?
A rich Benefits Package including Medical, Dental, Vision, Long and Short-Term Disability (Company Paid), a robust Wellness Program, tuition reimbursement, and a 401(k) Retirement Plan with matching.
Vacation and Sick Time
Up to Ten (10) paid holidays (including one diversity holiday)
Perfect Attendance and Employee Referral Bonus opportunities (up to $200 per referral)
Opportunities for growth and job training, for energetic, career-minded individuals.
Located in Saline, MI just minutes from Planet Fitness, Emagine Saline, and hundreds of local businesses!

Join our team and play a key role in delivering operational excellence and dependable service to our customers.

MMI Engineered Solutions is an equal opportunity employer

This role is central to ensuring excellent service and on-time delivery for our customers. You will serve as the primary point of contact, working cross-functionally with production, scheduling, sales, and engineering to support customer satisfaction and operational excellence.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Act as the main contact for assigned customer accounts, managing all communication and support needs

  • Coordinate and communicate changes to orders or delivery timelines with customers and internal stakeholders.
  • Understand and communicate customer-specific requirements across the organization (packaging, shipping, labeling, ordering, etc.).

  • Manage customer sales orders in the ERP system, ensuring data accuracy and timely processing.

  • Review and resolve daily EDI order issues; monitor and maintain customer portals as needed.

  • Maintain communication with the production scheduler to align delivery schedules and production priorities.

  • Monitor finished goods inventory and recommend adjustments to support customer demand.

  • Track and report on key metrics such as on-time delivery, backlog status, and performance trends.

  • Support product launches and the setup of new items, shipping locations, and routing requirements.

  • Assist with the development of order processing procedures and contingency plans.

  • Support continuous improvement efforts by identifying service gaps and proposing solutions.

Required Qualifications:

  • Bachelor's degree in Business Administration, Supply Chain, or a related field or 5+ years of relevant experience.

  • Strong background in customer service, preferably in manufacturing or the automotive industry.

  • Proactive mindset with a focus on problem-solving and customer satisfaction.
  • Excellent communication skills-both written and verbal.
  • Detail-oriented with strong organizational and multitasking abilities.

  • Familiarity with ERP systems, EDI processing, and sales order management.

  • Experience with OEM portals (e.g., Ford, GM, Stellantis, Mack/Volvo) is a plus.

  • Knowledge of EDI standards (X12 and EDIFACT) preferred.

Work EnvironmentExpectations:

  • Office-based role with occasional time spent in the manufacturing environment.

  • Minimal physical demands; primarily desk/clerical work.

  • Stop any shipment if customer requirements or quality concerns arise.
  • Maintain high levels of customer satisfaction and operational accuracy.

Why work at MMI?

  • A rich Benefits Package including Medical, Dental, Vision, Long and Short-Term Disability (Company Paid), a robust Wellness Program, tuition reimbursement, and a 401(k) Retirement Plan with matching.
  • Vacation and Sick Time
  • Up to Ten (10) paid holidays (including one diversity holiday)
  • Perfect Attendance and Employee Referral Bonus opportunities (up to $200 per referral)
  • Opportunities for growth and job training, for energetic, career-minded individuals.
  • Located in Saline, MI just minutes from Planet Fitness, Emagine Saline, and hundreds of local businesses!

Join our team and play a key role in delivering operational excellence and dependable service to our customers.

MMI Engineered Solutions is an equal opportunity employer

enrichment.score_rationale: The role has a highly repeatable workflow (ERP order management, EDI processing, OEM portal monitoring) common across manufacturing CSR roles, and several tasks like order entry, metric tracking, and EDI resolution are automation-friendly; however, cross-functional coordination, problem-solving, and direct customer communication introduce meaningful judgment requirements. System specificity scores only 1 as no named ERP (e.g., SAP, Oracle) is specified, and salary is not listed so cannot be confirmed above the $50K threshold.

description:
Accelerate the possible by joining a winning Amcor team that’s transforming the packaging industry and improving lives around the world.

At Amcor, we unpack possibility through our innovative and responsible packaging to provide solutions that benefit our customers, our people and our planet. More than 10,000 consumers worldwide encounter our products every second and rely on us for safe access to food, medicine and other goods. We value their trust by making safety our guiding principle. It’s our core value and integral to how we do business.

Beyond this core principle, our shared values and behaviors unite us as we work together to elevate customers, shape lives and protect the future. We champion our customers and help them succeed. We play to win – adapting quickly in an everchanging world – and make smart choices to safeguard our business, our communities and the people we serve for generations to come. And we invest in our world-class team, empowering our colleagues to unpack their potential, because we believe when our people grow, so does our business.

To learn more about playing for Team Amcor, visit www.amcor.com I LinkedIn I Glassdoor I Facebook I YouTube

Job Description

This role is the central point of contact between external Customers and the Amcor organization. Our Customers recognize this role as the “Face of Amcor”.

This is the first of three levels of Account Specialists within the Customer Service group. The level I has the basic knowledge and abilities to perform the below responsibilities and will initially require support to handle issues that may arise out of the daily responsibilities of the role.

WHAT YOU GET TO DO

Geographic Scope: North America
Number of colleagues directly reporting to this job: None

Customer Engagement

Build customer loyalty through positive customer engagement
Work in collaboration with other functions on escalated issues relating to customer orders, graphics issues, ship dates, quality concerns and more to meet customer expectations
Participate in conversations with customer regarding terms, agreements, purchase orders, inventory, and order status; asks for assistance from Sales or fellow Customer Account Specialists.
Follow standard processes and comply with service level agreements to support our customer-centric organization. Seek out learning opportunities and critical experiences to broaden understanding of standard work and best practices
Build initial knowledge of cross functional technical expertise, technical knowledge of products, order management systems and manufacturing processes.

Order Processing

Receive and process customer orders while screening information for accuracy and completeness
Monitor customer orders throughout the manufacturing process, advising customers and Sales of any significant delays and provides alternatives to ensure customer expectations are fulfilled

Shipping, Delivery & Inventory Coordination

Initiates customer releases for all shipments and ensures delivery dates are met as well as coordinates the expediting of rush shipments and monitors their progress
Manage and process outside warehousing shipments of inventory
Have a basic understanding that Amcor exports products to our customers
Manage sub-contracting business
Enter and manage non-conformances in a timely manner. Provide and incorporate corrective action plan for Customer Account Specialists owned non-conformances
Follow standard process to manage aged inventory
Manage consignment inventory and understand downstream affect
Manage basic Minimum and Maximum inventory programs
Partner with Sales, Marketing, and Product Data to create new material masters. Ensure all relevant documentation provided is accurate & continually maintained
Provide information to customers as requested such as: Bill of Lading, Proof of Delivery, Certificate of Compliance, Billings, and Inventory reports

Customer Account Maintenance

Maintain and actively utilize active customer electronic account profiles and item list to provide service excellence.

Miscellaneous

Support Continuous Improvement initiatives to determine new, improved and streamlined approaches to better serve customer & business needs.
Act as the back-up for designated team members. Maintain the same level of service excellence as provided to your core customers.

WHAT WE VALUE

Champion a culture of quality
Strong communication skills utilized in both internal and external relationships with a customer focus
Self-drive and initiative to seek out continuous improvement opportunities
Strong attention to detail and time management to best prioritize workload
Results Orientation, Strategic Orientation, Collaboration and Influence, Decision Making & Problem Solving

WHAT WE WANT FROM YOU

Bachelor’s Degree Preferred
Experience in a Manufacturing and/or Customer Service environment preferred
Basic Microsoft Office skills
Regular attendance
Frequent interaction with others
Ability to sit up to 100% of time
Ability to communicate up to 100% of time
Ability to move from one place to another up to 50% of time
Ability to work on a computer 100% of time
Ability to travel domestically 5% of time
Office environment

Our Expectations

We expect our people to be guided by The Amcor Way and demonstrate our Values every day to enable the business to win. We are winning when:

Our people are engaged and developing as part of a high-performing Amcor team
Our customers grow and prosper from Amcor’s quality, service, and innovation
Our investors benefit from Amcor’s consistent growth and superior returns
The environment is better off because of Amcor’s leadership and products

Equal Opportunity Employer/Minorities/Females/Disabled/Veterans/Sexual Orientation/Gender Identity

Amcor is an Equal Opportunity Employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability status, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

If you would like more information about your EEO rights as an applicant under the law, please click on the "Know Your Rights: Workplace Discrimination is Illegal" Poster. If, because of a medical condition or disability, you need a reasonable accommodation for any part of the employment process, please call 224-313-7000 and let us know the nature of your request and your contact information.

E-Verify

We verify the identity and employment authorization of individuals hired for employment in the United States.

Benefits

When you join Amcor, you will have access to a comprehensive benefits and compensation package that includes:

Medical, dental and vision plans

Flexible time off, starting at 80 hours paid time per year for full-time salaried employees

Company-paid holidays starting at 8 days per year and may vary by location

Wellbeing program & Employee Assistance Program

Health Savings Account/Flexible Spending Account

Life insurance, AD&D, short-term & long-term disability, and voluntary benefits

Paid Parental Leave

Retirement Savings Plan with company match

Tuition Reimbursement (dependent upon approval)

Discretionary annual bonus program (initial eligibility dependent upon hire date)

description:
Company Description

Williams Lea by RRD is a global business support services company with a strong legacy—over 200 years of experience delivering world-class business solutions. We specialize in delivering skilled administrative support, document production, presentation design, and marketing and communications services to leading companies around the world—especially within legal, financial, and professional services industries.

We’re a people-powered organization. With a presence in North America, the UK, Europe, and Asia Pacific, we have thousands of employees globally, all working together to help our clients operate more efficiently and effectively. Whether it’s supporting law firms with critical document production or helping financial institutions manage high-volume print and digital communications, we’re the behind-the-scenes team making everything run smoothly

Job Description

The Document Production Associate position is responsible for providing word processing and/or document production services for our clients.

(* denotes an “essential function”)

*Utilize appropriate logs and/or tracking software for all document production work
*Thoroughly assess job request and ensure appropriate completion of job tasks throughout task lifecycle
*Perform work in document production, proofreading, intake and workflow coordination functions, as needed
*Use established procedures, standards and formats to edit, proof, convert, create, transcribe or otherwise complete document production requests to client satisfaction
*Demonstrate proficiency in using equipment/technology/software and hardware necessary to perform job functions
*Communicate with team members, lead, supervisor or client on job or deadline concerns
*Meet contracted deadlines for service delivery to our clients
*Troubleshoot basic software or hardware problems
Help to foster a proactive environment of continuous service enhancement and relationship building with the client
Perform quality assurance on work, as requested
Adhere to Williams Lea Tag policies, in addition to client policies
Use equipment and supplies in a cost efficient manner

Qualifications

High school diploma or equivalent
Minimum (1) year document production experience preferably in a legal, banking or large corporate environment
Skilled in the use MS Office software (Word, Excel, PowerPoint); strong keyboarding and typing skills
Familiar with other software programs for editing and/or creating documents
Strong attention to detail; able to work on multiple projects simultaneously
Must have good organizational skills
Must be able to meet deadlines and complete all projects in a timely manner
Ability to handle sensitive and/or confidential documents and information
Able to exercise good judgment to make decisions that conform to business needs and policy
Good problem solving skills, with the ability and understanding of when to escalate a problem to a supervisory level
Ability to maintain professional composure when working with immediate deadlines
Ability to work both independently and collaboratively as part of a team
Ability to work in a fast paced environment
Ability to communicate professionally both verbally and in writing
Must be self-motivated with a positive attitude
Proven customer service skills are required in order to create, maintain and enhance customer relationships

Additional Information

The salary for this role at the noted RRD location is $17.25 / hour. Starting pay decisions
are determined based on multiple factors including but not limited to relevant education, qualifications,
skills, experience, certifications, proficiency, performance, shift, location, and other business
needs. Typically, roles follow step progressions to a target rate or set increments over time. Depending on
the role, in addition to the hourly rate of pay, the total compensation package may also include overtime, shift differential, call-in, and/or stand-by pay. RRD’s benefit offerings include
medical, dental, and vision coverage, paid time off, disability insurance, 401(k) with company match, life
insurance and other voluntary supplemental insurance coverages, plus parental leave, adoption
assistance, tuition assistance and employer/partner discounts.

#WLWV

All employment offers are contingent upon the successful completion of both a pre-employment background and drug screen.

RRD is an Equal Opportunity Employer, including disability/veterans

enrichment.score_rationale: Tasks are highly repetitive and rule-based (editing, converting, proofreading, formatting documents using established standards), and the workflow is essentially identical across hundreds of legal/financial support shops — but the role only explicitly names MS Office (no API-rich tools) and pay comes in just under the $35K threshold at ~$35,880/$17.25/hr.
core_tasks: Utilize logs and tracking software for document production work
Assess job requests and manage task lifecycle completion
Edit, proof, convert, create, or transcribe documents to client specifications
Perform intake and workflow coordination functions
Conduct quality assurance on completed work
Troubleshoot basic software and hardware problems
Communicate with team and clients on job status and deadlines
Meet contracted service delivery deadlines
Adhere to client and company policies for handling confidential documents

description:
Company Description

Williams Lea by RRD is a global business support services company with a strong legacy—over 200 years of experience delivering world-class business solutions. We specialize in delivering skilled administrative support, document production, presentation design, and marketing and communications services to leading companies around the world—especially within legal, financial, and professional services industries.

We’re a people-powered organization. With a presence in North America, the UK, Europe, and Asia Pacific, we have thousands of employees globally, all working together to help our clients operate more efficiently and effectively. Whether it’s supporting law firms with critical document production or helping financial institutions manage high-volume print and digital communications, we’re the behind-the-scenes team making everything run smoothly

Job Description

The Document Production Associate position is responsible for providing word processing and/or document production services for our clients.

(* denotes an “essential function”)

*Utilize appropriate logs and/or tracking software for all document production work
*Thoroughly assess job request and ensure appropriate completion of job tasks throughout task lifecycle
*Perform work in document production, proofreading, intake and workflow coordination functions, as needed
*Use established procedures, standards and formats to edit, proof, convert, create, transcribe or otherwise complete document production requests to client satisfaction
*Demonstrate proficiency in using equipment/technology/software and hardware necessary to perform job functions
*Communicate with team members, lead, supervisor or client on job or deadline concerns
*Meet contracted deadlines for service delivery to our clients
*Troubleshoot basic software or hardware problems
Help to foster a proactive environment of continuous service enhancement and relationship building with the client
Perform quality assurance on work, as requested
Adhere to Williams Lea Tag policies, in addition to client policies
Use equipment and supplies in a cost efficient manner

Qualifications

High school diploma or equivalent
Minimum (1) year document production experience preferably in a legal, banking or large corporate environment
Skilled in the use MS Office software (Word, Excel, PowerPoint); strong keyboarding and typing skills
Familiar with other software programs for editing and/or creating documents
Strong attention to detail; able to work on multiple projects simultaneously
Must have good organizational skills
Must be able to meet deadlines and complete all projects in a timely manner
Ability to handle sensitive and/or confidential documents and information
Able to exercise good judgment to make decisions that conform to business needs and policy
Good problem solving skills, with the ability and understanding of when to escalate a problem to a supervisory level
Ability to maintain professional composure when working with immediate deadlines
Ability to work both independently and collaboratively as part of a team
Ability to work in a fast paced environment
Ability to communicate professionally both verbally and in writing
Must be self-motivated with a positive attitude
Proven customer service skills are required in order to create, maintain and enhance customer relationships

Additional Information

The salary for this role at the noted RRD location is $17 / hour. Starting pay decisions are determined based on multiple factors including but not limited to relevant education, qualifications,
skills, experience, certifications, proficiency, performance, shift, location, and other business
needs. Typically, roles follow step progressions to a target rate or set increments over time. Depending on
the role, in addition to the hourly rate of pay, the total compensation package may also include overtime, shift differential, call-in, and/or stand-by pay. RRD’s benefit offerings include
medical, dental, and vision coverage, paid time off, disability insurance, 401(k) with company match, life
insurance and other voluntary supplemental insurance coverages, plus parental leave, adoption
assistance, tuition assistance and employer/partner discounts.

#WLWV

All employment offers are contingent upon the successful completion of both a pre-employment background and drug screen.

RRD is an Equal Opportunity Employer, including disability/veterans

description:
Wolters Kluwer CT-division provided compliance and registered agent services to our clients. Registered agent services - includes a registered agent location within the state to receive legal and critical business communications and route all service of process legal notifications

As a Fulfillment Associate, Document Management, you will play a vital role in ensuring customer orders are accurately processed on time. You'll contribute to streamlining order entry, administration, and shipping processes to deliver exceptional customer service. This starting point-level position provides an excellent opportunity to begin a career by supporting the team and learning foundational skills.

In this role, you will report to the Manager, Fulfillment and work from our local office in Wilmington, DE. This is a work from the office with a M-F schedule (8:30 AM-5:00 PM).

Key Responsibilities:

• Receive documents (via served, certified, regular mail, etc.) prep, batch and scan said documents sent to CT in our capacity as registered agent as well as interact with process servers, sheriffs and any other individuals delivering documents to CT Offices.

• Work collaboratively and directly with internal customers to deliver solutions that exceed customer expectations and minimize customer impacts.

• Ability to determine any issues that require escalating and direct to appropriate support teams.

• Engage in training to utilize internal systems and tools (e.g., ARROW); and becoming proficient in dashboard management and operating scanning tools.

• Adhere to Service of Process standard operating procedures.

• Ability to meet the required metric for the position (Production; Quality and Timeliness).

Qualifications:

• High School Diploma or equivalent. College degree preferred.

• 1+ years' experience in an office or customer service role. Registered Agent Services, Legal, and Government support experience preferred.

• Ability to lift up to 30 Lbs.

• Navigate Department of State websites

• Microsoft Office, CRM, database

• Email Etiquette

• Stable work history

Preferred Knowledge/Experience:

• Registered Agent Services

• UPS Campus ship, Kodak Capture Pro - i5000

• Navigate Department of State Websites

• Email Etiquette• Unitech Scanner Gun

• Lift Up to 30 Lbs.

• #LI-Office

Our Interview Practices

To maintain a fair and genuine hiring process, we kindly ask that all candidates participate in interviews without the assistance of AI tools or external prompts. Our interview process is designed to assess your individual skills, experiences, and communication style. We value authenticity and want to ensure we’re getting to know you—not a digital assistant. To help maintain this integrity, we ask to remove virtual backgrounds and include in-person interviews in our hiring process. Please note that use of AI-generated responses or third-party support during interviews will be grounds for disqualification from the recruitment process.

Applicants may be required to appear onsite at a Wolters Kluwer office as part of the recruitment process.

Compensation:

$27,700.00 - $46,450.00 USD

Compensation range listed is based on primary location of the position. Actual base salary offer is influenced by a wide array of factors including but not limited to skills, experience and actual hiring location. Your recruiter can share more information about the specific offer for the job location during the hiring process.

Additional Information:

Wolters Kluwer offers a wide variety of competitive benefits and programs to help meet your needs and balance your work and personal life, including but not limited to: Medical, Dental, & Vision Plans, 401(k), FSA/HSA, Commuter Benefits, Tuition Assistance Plan, Vacation and Sick Time, and Paid Parental Leave. Full details of our benefits are available upon request.

Collaborate with internal customers to resolve issues

positionName: Associate Analyst, Accessory Supply (2 Month Temp Assignment)
description:
Associate Analyst, Accessory Supply (2 Month Temp Assignment)

We are searching for an experienced Associate Analyst, Accessory Supply (2 Month Temp Assignment) at our Headquarters facility.

Hyundai MOBIS Parts America

We think creatively and keep challenging ourselves to help create a new future and eventually make humankind’s dreams a reality. Ready for the challenge?

Who We Are

Tomorrow's most advanced automotive technology is being envisioned today at MOBIS Parts America (MPA). A subsidiary of Hyundai MOBIS, we are a top leading automotive parts supplier. MPA provides after-sales service parts support for Hyundai Motors, Kia Motors, and Genesis. In addition to the distribution of genuine service parts, the MPA Accessories division develops accessories for Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis - which are installed on new vehicles in the United States and Canada.

Description

The Associate Accessory Supply Analyst will assist in ensuring parts availability to support OE service and accessory parts requirements at dealer and port facilities. This role focuses on minimizing customer impact by expediting orders and resolving backorder situations. The Associate Analyst will gain experience in communicating with OE's, service partners, and vendors and will contribute to the improvement of standard operating procedures (SOP's). This role is an excellent opportunity to develop skills in communication, organization, and problem-solving, while supporting the efficient operation of the accessory supply chain.

 KEY ACCOUNTABILITIES

Review dealer backorder file and provide written updates daily,
Enter transfers necessary to avoid unnecessary referrals,
Expediting orders from PDC for port shortages.
Review port install errors in AMOS.
Create part transfers to replenish inventory weekly for ports,
Place supply orders to ensure stable pipeline for assigned parts,
Meet with ports to review and clear errors.
Assist in the review and documentation of SOPs
Support communication with Management of MPA and OEs on business KPls, current statuses, and issues and resolutions to provide better customer service.
Work closely with customers (OEs) and vendors to support business and improve processes.
KEY PERFORMANCE INDICATORS
 Number of backorders resolved daily
 Time taken to process orders
 Accuracy of inventory transfersCompleteness of port shortage reports

 WORK SCHEDULE/TRAVEL

8:00 am - 5:00 pm (or as approved by management)
Minimal overnight travel (up to 10%) by land and/or air.

Required Qualifications

EDUCATION REQUIREMENTS

 High School Diploma is required.
EXPERIENCE REQUIREMENTS
0-1 years of experience in a related filed preferred
Proficient in basic computer skills and software packages (word processing, spreadsheets)
Strong organizational skills and attention to detail

Employment Type - Non-Exempt

Compensation - Hourly Wage , $47,273.00 - 69,727.67

Hyundai Mobis Parts America - Benefits

We offer a competitive benefits package for full-time employees which include:

Medical, Dental, and Vision
401K with employer matching
Tuition Reimbursement
Paid Vacation, Paid Sick Days and Paid Holidays
Company Discounts & more!

Our company provides equal employment opportunities (EEO) to all employees and applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability or genetics.

description:
Now Hiring: Customer Service Representative
Troy, OH | Monday–Friday, 8:00 AM–5:00 PM | Hourly, Non‑Exempt
Why You’ll Love This Role
At Duraco Laminated Solutions, our Customer Service Representatives are the heartbeat of our customer relationships. If you enjoy solving problems, collaborating with multiple teams, and making a real impact on customer satisfaction every day, this role is for you.
You’ll be part of a supportive, fast‑paced manufacturing environment where accuracy, communication, and teamwork matter—and where your contributions are genuinely valued.
What You’ll Do
As a Customer Service Representative, you’ll be responsible for ensuring clear, accurate, and timely communication between our customers, plant operations, and the account management team. Your day‑to‑day work will include:
Building and maintaining strong, positive relationships with customers
Processing customer orders with a high level of accuracy and efficiency
Communicating customer requests, concerns, and ship date changes to the appropriate departments
Resolving customer issues related to pricing, quality, or service
Partnering with Account Managers and technical teams on special projects
Providing information and guidance on pricing, procedures, and company policies
Maintaining daily order reports and ensuring all orders are entered into the CRM system
Preparing quotes and obtaining raw material pricing
Coordinating requests for samples, brochures, and product literature
Supporting the Customer Service Manager as needed
Managing Vendor Managed Inventory (VMI) for key accounts
Works with Account Managers and the technical team on special projects, and supplies information and advice on pricing, procedures, and policies.

What We’re Looking For
We’re seeking someone who is detail‑oriented, customer‑focused, and comfortable working in a manufacturing environment.
What Will Help You Succeed:
Customer service experience—manufacturing experience is a plus, but not required
Comfort using computers and programs like Excel, Word, or CRM systems (or willingness to learn)
Strong communication skills, organization, and attention to detail
A positive attitude, reliability, and a team‑first mindset
✨ Training is provided, and we’re excited to support your growth at Duraco.
Key Skills & Competencies:
Strong problem‑solving abilities and attention to detail
Excellent customer service and communication skills (verbal and written)
Ability to handle challenging situations with professionalism and tact
Strong organizational and time‑management skills
Adaptability in a fast‑paced, changing environment
Dependable attendance and reliability
Proficiency with basic math, including fractions and decimals
Preferred Experience
Experience with the following systems or tools is a plus:
CRM systems or database software
Inventory and manufacturing systems
Order processing systems
Experience with Microsoft Excel, Word, and CRM systems
Physical Requirements
This role is primarily office‑based. Employees must be able to sit for extended periods and occasionally stand, walk, and reach. Light lifting (up to 10 lbs.) may be required. Reasonable accommodation may be provided for individuals with disabilities.
Work Environment
This position is performed in a fast‑paced office setting with frequent interruptions. The noise level is typically low to moderate.
Are You Ready to Apply?
If you’re looking for a role where your organizational skills, customer focus, and problem‑solving abilities can shine—we’d love to hear from you. Join a team where your work truly makes a difference.
#NowHiring #CustomerServiceJobs #ManufacturingCareers #Duraco #TroyOH

Whether it’s supporting law firms with critical document production or helping financial institutions manage high-volume print and digital communications, we’re the behind-the-scenes team making everything run smoothly.

Job Description

The Document Production Associate position is responsible for providing word processing and/or document production services for our clients.

(* denotes an “essential function”)

*Utilize appropriate logs and/or tracking software for all document production work
*Thoroughly assess job request and ensure appropriate completion of job tasks throughout task lifecycle
*Perform work in document production, proofreading, intake and workflow coordination functions, as needed
*Use established procedures, standards and formats to edit, proof, convert, create, transcribe or otherwise complete document production requests to client satisfaction
*Demonstrate proficiency in using equipment/technology/software and hardware necessary to perform job functions
*Communicate with team members, lead, supervisor or client on job or deadline concerns
*Meet contracted deadlines for service delivery to our clients
*Troubleshoot basic software or hardware problems
Help to foster a proactive environment of continuous service enhancement and relationship building with the client
Perform quality assurance on work, as requested
Adhere to Williams Lea Tag policies, in addition to client policies
Use equipment and supplies in a cost efficient manner

Qualifications

High school diploma or equivalent
Minimum (1) year document production experience preferably in a legal, banking or large corporate environment
Skilled in the use MS Office software (Word, Excel, PowerPoint); strong keyboarding and typing skills
Familiar with other software programs for editing and/or creating documents
Strong attention to detail; able to work on multiple projects simultaneously
Must have good organizational skills
Must be able to meet deadlines and complete all projects in a timely manner
Ability to handle sensitive and/or confidential documents and information
Able to exercise good judgment to make decisions that conform to business needs and policy
Good problem solving skills, with the ability and understanding of when to escalate a problem to a supervisory level
Ability to maintain professional composure when working with immediate deadlines
Ability to work both independently and collaboratively as part of a team
Ability to work in a fast paced environment
Ability to communicate professionally both verbally and in writing
Must be self-motivated with a positive attitude
Proven customer service skills are required in order to create, maintain and enhance customer relationships

Additional Information

The salary range for this role at the noted RRD location is $16.50- $17.50 / hour. Starting pay decisions
are determined based on multiple factors including but not limited to relevant education, qualifications,
skills, experience, certifications, proficiency, performance, shift, location, and other business
needs. Typically, roles follow step progressions to a target rate or set increments over time. Depending on
the role, in addition to the hourly rate of pay, the total compensation package may also include overtime, shift differential, call-in, and/or stand-by pay. RRD’s benefit offerings include
medical, dental, and vision coverage, paid time off, disability insurance, 401(k) with company match, life
insurance and other voluntary supplemental insurance coverages, plus parental leave, adoption
assistance, tuition assistance and employer/partner discounts.

#LI-onsite

#WLWV

All employment offers are contingent upon the successful completion of both a pre-employment background and drug screen.

RRD is an Equal Opportunity Employer, including disability/veterans

description:
Job Summary
This position will work closely with all IEEE Account Managers, Inside Sales Reps, International Dealers, Subscription agents and end user customers on a daily basis, in both domestic and International markets. In 2025, the Order Management team processed over 5,700 orders, valued at more than $328M in IEEE revenue

This position reports to the Senior Manager, Order Management, and functions as an individual contributor. This position must be able to work independently in a fast-paced, deadline-oriented environment, and is expected to recommend changes in department procedures/processes, as well as suggesting improvements to shared processes with departments outside of Customer Operations.

Once trained and deemed proficient in their job, the employee may be eligible to work in a hybrid office/remote structure that includes periodic telecommuting in accordance with IEEE and departmental practices. This is subject to change at IEEE's sole discretion

The role provides access to over 75 different online packages and 200+ online journals new/renewal online subscription orders. Additionally, Order management processes print/single journal subscription titles, print claims, proforma invoices, generates credits and refunds, IP amendments, cancellations, processes trial requests for potential new customers, participates in quarterly system testing as well as any other system testing needed throughout the year and may witness document signing and notarize documents.

The individual will be assigned International and Domestic territories that is well over a million dollars in IEEE revenue. Therefore, attention to detail and accuracy are critical to the success of this position. This individual will build a strong working relationship with the IEEE Dealers, IEEE Account managers, IEEE Sales reps as well as other key IEEE departments that include:
• Finance and Administration-Credit & Collections
• Finance & Administration-Facilities/Distribution
• Finance & Administration-Tax Management
• All levels of Sales Management
• Pubs IT
• Product design with new products as well as latest pricing models for existent
• IEEE Computer Society
• IEEE ComSoc
While periodic overtime will be necessary, the influx of orders submitted between October and February will require more significant support to ensure the timely and accurate processing of orders within the monthly and year-end fiscal calendar that will impact the IEEE revenue stream and account manager and dealer commissions

Key Responsibilities

Dynamic auditing of order submissions to ensure consistent and accurate information necessary to execute 90+ decision paths and fields with every order
Responsible for interacting with all levels of sales and sales management for additional information or escalations if needed
Protects IEEE intellectual properties by verifying each individual IPv4/v6 address(s) to ensure only authorized users receive access to subscribed content; This process includes verifying the IP(s) in approved software that will determine if the IP domain belongs to the customer; Sets customer’s online access in our Rightscare software with the given IP’s, product license(s), term dates to ensure the customer has access to their subscription(s); Rejects and/or questions submitted IP amendments if ownership cannot be validated.
Reviews purchase orders to ensure they meet IEEE standards, and determine if the purchase order needs to be endorsed by a manager or reviewed further through the IEEE legal team; If legal needs to review the Purchase Order, works closely with the IEEE Customer Licensing team
Handles Consortia accounts that may contain hundreds of participating Academia schools; Access may need to be created in several groups to ensure the Academia schools have the proper access to their subscription(s)
In situations where there is inaccuracy in the submitted order, the analyst will make recommendations to the department management whether or not the order should be accepted or rejected
Conducts monthly Quality Assurance audits for team members
Meets or exceeds monthly/yearly individual metrics as well as department metrics that has been set by the manager
On those occasions when working remotely, the employee must maintain all quality and productivity standards to maintain hybrid remote eligibility. To that end, employees must sign into IEEE systems promptly at the beginning of their assigned shifts, and they must remain in continuous communication with their manager and coworkers throughout the business day via the methods made available to them including personal and group chat, text, email, and/or telephone.
Responsible for in house department procedures to ensure they are up to standards
Serves as primary tester for new integrations and/or software
Works on special projects & data reports as needed

Qualifications

Education

H.S. diploma Req
Bachelor's degree or equivalent experience Pref
Work Experience

2-4 years professional experience Req
Order processing experience Pref
Skills and Requirements

Demonstrated ability to learn and effectively use computer-based business systems. Prior experience with SalesForce Lightning, Siebel, Rightscare, Vertex, and Oracle AR financials are highly desirable
Knowledge of Microsoft programs, especially Word, Excel, Powerpoint
Knowledge of Gmail, Google Docs, and Google Drive
Positive, customer-focused attitude in dealing with a variety of external /internal customers
Strong organizational skills
Must be able to effectively collaborate with multiple organizational units
Ability to handle multiple projects simultaneously, and track and report status of each
Ability to work under pressure in a fast-paced environment
Strong-time management skills to balance demands and meet deadlines
Able to work equally effectively in the office as when working remotely (if applicable)
Positive, proactive work attitude and ability to excel in fast-moving work environment with changing priorities
Cooperative ability to work with other staff to address any issues
Professional demeanor with the ability to handle confidential material
Present sensitivity to foreign cultures and mannerisms
Must be a technologically savvy knowledge worker who seeks out technology solutions challenges
Must be able to travel less than 5%
Must be able to work partial evening shifts on a periodic basis to assist staff located in IEEE International offices (Singapore and India)
Other Requirements:
As defined in IEEE Policies, individuals currently serving on an IEEE board or committee are not eligible to apply.

PLEASE NOTE: This position is not budgeted for employer-sponsored immigration support, this includes all persons in F (both CPT and OPT), J, H, L, or O status.

For information on work demands and conditions required for this position, please consult the reference document, ADA Requirements. This position is classified under Category I - Office Positions.

All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, or status as a protected veteran.
===============================================
Disclaimer: This job description is proprietary to IEEE. It outlines the general nature and key features performed by various positions that share the same job classification. It is not designed to contain or be interpreted as a comprehensive inventory of all duties and qualifications required of all employees assigned to the job. Nothing in this job description restricts management’s right to assign or re-assign duties to this job at any time due to reasonable accommodations or other business reasons.

description:
Description

Work From Home positions available in the Boise, ID area. We are seeking a Customer Order Specialist to work in our Central Service Center. This position will play a key role for the Customer Order Team by driving standardization and efficiency of process. This role will provide excellent customer service and process customer orders within the Shared Services center.

This position is responsible for order entry processing and will utilize policies and procedures to consistently ensure an excellent customer experience. This position troubleshoots and resolves customer complaints and disputes and is responsible for assisting with continual process improvements and cost-effective delivery of services.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
Resolves customer inquiries by utilizing SAP and departmental resources
Develops and maintains documentation on all assigned customers/business units/locations
Researches and works across functional areas to identify and resolve order accuracy, product availability, and order timeliness
Processes standard orders received by customer or field sales (via telephone, fax, EDI, e-mail) including ensuring the invoicing of shipments in a timely and efficient manner
Processes EDI orders, resolving any errors on both the inbound order, and outbound subsequent documents
Communicates effectively with distribution facilities to ensure on-time shipment and delivery of customer orders, thus maintaining appropriate service levels
Works effectively and professionally with the customer and with internal functional areas, to include, but not limited to: Distribution, Credit, Accounts Receivable, Sales & Operations Planning
Delivers on defined operational goals and development plans, keeping Supervisor informed on progress, and ensures timely and accurate completion of work to goals.
Supports RE025 SOX Audit control with weekly performance of self- and partner- order audits
Performs other assigned tasks and duties necessary to support the Order Management team

QUALIFICATIONS

Consumer Products experience preferred
Experience working with EDI preferred
Experience working with enterprise systems such as SAP, Oracle, AS400, A+
Experience multi- tasking and resolving problems in a professional manner
Works well in an environment with firm deadlines; results oriented
Strong customer service and communication skills
Polite and positive phone demeanor
Strong written, verbal, interpersonal and analytical skills
Strong attention to detail, goal oriented
Proficient in Microsoft Outlook and Excel
Perform multiple tasks effectively; prioritize and respond with appropriate sense of urgency
Able to work both independently and as part of a team

MINIMUM EXPERIENCE & EDUCATION

3+ years of multi-product Customer Service experience and/or previous sales experience.
Experience working with large customer base and high volume of transactions
Experience working with Microsoft Office Products is necessary (Excel, Outlook)

WORKING CONDITIONS
Corporate office environment or home office with frequent use of phone and PC
New Employee training is held in the office. WFH is offered after training is complete

BENEFITS PACKAGE & EMPLOYEE PROGRAMS

Comprehensive Medical, Dental, and Vision Insurance
Free Life and Disability Insurance
Health and Dependent Care Flexible Spending Accounts
401k with 3% company match and annual employer discretionary contribution
Paid vacation, holidays and sick time
Employee Assistance Program
Access to thousands of free online courses
Discounts on cell phones, movie tickets, gym memberships, and more!
Education Assistance (both college degrees and professional certifications)
Referral Program with cash bonus
Access to on-demand pay
Paid parental leave

Central Garden & Pet Company (NASDAQ: CENT) (NASDAQ: CENTA) is a leading consumer goods company in the pet and garden industries. Guided by the belief that home is central to life, the Company's purpose is to proudly nurture happy and healthy homes. For over 45 years, its innovative and trusted solutions have helped lawns grow greener, gardens bloom bigger, pets live healthier, and communities grow stronger. Central is home to a diversified portfolio of market-leading brands including Amdro®, Aqueon®, Best Bully Sticks®, Cadet®, C&S®, Farnam®, Ferry-Morse®, Kaytee®, Nylabone®, Pennington®, Sevin® and Zoёcon®. With fiscal 2025 net sales of $3.1 billion, Central has strong manufacturing and logistics capabilities supported by a passionate, entrepreneurial growth culture. The Company is headquartered in Walnut Creek, California, and employs over 6,000 people, primarily across North America. Visit www.central.com to learn more.

We are an equal opportunity employer, and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability status, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity, or any other characteristic protected by law.

description:
JOB ID: 723552BR
Date posted: Apr. 14, 2026

Description:You will be a Manufacturing Planner Associate to support production planning and material readiness for our Camden operations. This role is ideal for candidates looking to build foundational experience in production planning, inventory control, and supply chain coordination within a fast-paced manufacturing environment.

What You Will Be Doing

You will partner closely with Quality, Purchasing, Warehousing, and Production teams to ensure materials and schedules align to meet program demands.

Key Responsibilities
• Monitor production schedules and ensure work orders are released on time to support program needs
• Create and maintain reports to track material status, shortages, and schedule performance
• Coordinate daily material requirements with Purchasing and Expediting teams
• Investigate and resolve inventory discrepancies to maintain accurate system data
• Confirm material supply and demand to support uninterrupted production flow
• Communicate material availability risks and potential production impacts to program and operations teams
• Support stock transfers and inter-site material movements as needed
• Assist in resolving shortages, over-shipments, engineering changes, and traceability concerns
• Maintain and update MRP parameters (lead times, lot sizes, BOM changes) in coordination with Engineering and Change Control
• Assist in preparing and processing DD250 documentation to support product shipments and customer acceptance, ensuring required data and approvals are complete and accurate

What You’ll Gain
• Hands-on experience with ERP/MRP systems
• Exposure to production planning and manufacturing operations
• Cross-functional collaboration across supply chain and production
• A strong foundation for career growth within Planning & Control

Why Join Us
This role is ideal for candidates looking to build foundational experience in production planning, inventory control, and supply chain coordination within a fast-paced manufacturing environment.

We are committed to supporting your work-life balance and overall well-being, offering flexible scheduling options

Further Information About This Opportunity:
This position is in Camden. Discover more about our Camden, Arkansas location.

MUST BE A U.S. CITIZEN – This position is located at a facility that requires special access.
Basic Qualifications:
• Bachelor’s degree or relevant experience
• Strong organizational skills and attention to detail
• Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced manufacturing or operations environment
• Proficiency with Microsoft Excel and basic data analysis/reporting
• Effective communication skills and ability to work cross-functionally
• Ability to work full-time onsite in Camden on Third Shift
• MUST BE A U.S. CITIZEN – This position is located at a facility that requires special access.
Desired Skills:
• Experience working with ERP/MRP systems (SAP or similar)
• Exposure to inventory control, production planning, or material coordination
• Experience analyzing data or creating reports in Excel (pivot tables, lookups, etc.)
• Comfortable working directly with shop floor or production teams
• Experience resolving discrepancies or troubleshooting process issues
• Interest in developing a career in Production Planning & Control
Clearance Level:None
Other Important Information You Should Know
Expression of Interest:By applying to this job, you are expressing interest in this position and could be considered for other career opportunities where similar skills and requirements have been identified as a match. Should this match be identified you may be contacted for this and future openings.
Ability to Work Remotely:Onsite Full-time: The work associated with this position will be performed onsite at a designated Lockheed Martin facility.
Work Schedules:Lockheed Martin supports a variety of alternate work schedules that provide additional flexibility to our employees. Schedules range from standard 40 hours over a five day work week while others may be condensed. These condensed schedules provide employees with additional time away from the office and are in addition to our Paid Time off benefits.
Schedule for this Position:4x10 hour day, 3 days off per week
Lockheed Martin is an equal opportunity employer. Qualified candidates will be considered without regard to legally protected characteristics.
The application window will close in 90 days; applicants are encouraged to apply within 5 - 30 days of the requisition posting date in order to receive optimal consideration.
At Lockheed Martin, we use our passion for purposeful innovation to help keep people safe and solve the world's most complex challenges. Our people are some of the greatest minds in the industry and truly make Lockheed Martin a great place to work.

With our employees as our priority, we provide diverse career opportunities designed to propel, develop, and boost agility. Our flexible schedules, competitive pay, and comprehensive benefits enable our employees to live a healthy, fulfilling life at and outside of work. We place an emphasis on empowering our employees by fostering an inclusive environment built upon integrity and corporate responsibility.

If this sounds like a culture you connect with, you’re invited to apply for this role. Or, if you are unsure whether your experience aligns with the requirements of this position, we encourage you to search on Lockheed Martin Jobs, and apply for roles that align with your qualifications.

descriptionHTML:
JOB ID: 723552BR

Date posted: Apr. 14, 2026


Description:

You will be a Manufacturing Planner Associate to support production planning and material readiness for our Camden operations. This role is ideal for candidates looking to build foundational experience in production planning, inventory control, and supply chain coordination within a fast-paced manufacturing environment.


What You Will Be Doing


You will partner closely with Quality, Purchasing, Warehousing, and Production teams to ensure materials and schedules align to meet program demands.

Key Responsibilities
  • Monitor production schedules and ensure work orders are released on time to support program needs
  • Create and maintain reports to track material status, shortages, and schedule performance
  • Coordinate daily material requirements with Purchasing and Expediting teams
  • Investigate and resolve inventory discrepancies to maintain accurate system data
  • Confirm material supply and demand to support uninterrupted production flow
  • Communicate material availability risks and potential production impacts to program and operations teams
  • Support stock transfers and inter-site material movements as needed
  • Assist in resolving shortages, over-shipments, engineering changes, and traceability concerns
  • Maintain and update MRP parameters (lead times, lot sizes, BOM changes) in coordination with Engineering and Change Control
  • Assist in preparing and processing DD250 documentation to support product shipments and customer acceptance, ensuring required data and approvals are complete and accurate

What You’ll Gain
  • Hands-on experience with ERP/MRP systems
  • Exposure to production planning and manufacturing operations
  • Cross-functional collaboration across supply chain and production
  • A strong foundation for career growth within Planning & Control

Why Join Us


This role is ideal for candidates looking to build foundational experience in production planning, inventory control, and supply chain coordination within a fast-paced manufacturing environment.

We are committed to supporting your work-life balance and overall well-being, offering flexible scheduling options

Further Information About This Opportunity:


This position is in Camden. Discover more about our Camden, Arkansas location.

MUST BE A U.S. CITIZEN – This position is located at a facility that requires special access.

Basic Qualifications:


  • Bachelor’s degree or relevant experience
  • Strong organizational skills and attention to detail
  • Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced manufacturing or operations environment
  • Proficiency with Microsoft Excel and basic data analysis/reporting
  • Effective communication skills and ability to work cross-functionally
  • Ability to work full-time onsite in Camden on Third Shift
  • MUST BE A U.S. CITIZEN – This position is located at a facility that requires special access.

Desired Skills:


  • Experience working with ERP/MRP systems (SAP or similar)
  • Exposure to inventory control, production planning, or material coordination
  • Experience analyzing data or creating reports in Excel (pivot tables, lookups, etc.)
  • Comfortable working directly with shop floor or production teams
  • Experience resolving discrepancies or troubleshooting process issues
  • Interest in developing a career in Production Planning & Control

Clearance Level:

None

Other Important Information You Should Know


Expression of Interest:

By applying to this job, you are expressing interest in this position and could be considered for other career opportunities where similar skills and requirements have been identified as a match. Should this match be identified you may be contacted for this and future openings.

Ability to Work Remotely:

Onsite Full-time: The work associated with this position will be performed onsite at a designated Lockheed Martin facility.

Lockheed Martin supports a variety of alternate work schedules that provide additional flexibility to our employees. Schedules range from standard 40 hours over a five day work week while others may be condensed. These condensed schedules provide employees with additional time away from the office and are in addition to our Paid Time off benefits.

Schedule for this Position:

enrichment.score_rationale: Most tasks (schedule monitoring, MRP updates, shortage tracking, report creation) are rule-based and highly repeatable across manufacturing companies, but resolving discrepancies, cross-functional coordination, and traceability concerns require human judgment. Only SAP is named as a desired tool, and salary is not listed — Lockheed entry-level in Arkansas likely falls below $50K threshold for this associate role.
